title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
summary: Entrepreneurs in China are building businesses around OpenClaw, a popular open-source AI tool that can autonomously complete device tasks. The trend reflects growing opportunities for developers to create innovative applications leveraging emerging AI capabilities.
content: Feng Qingyang had always hoped to launch his own company, but he never thought this would be how—or that the day would come this fast.  Feng, a 27-year-old software engineer based in Beijing, started tinkering with OpenClaw, a popular new open-source AI tool that can take over a device and autonomously complete tasks for a…

zh.title: 投机者正在利用中国OpenClaw AI热潮赚钱
zh.summary: 中国企业家正在围绕OpenClaw这一流行的开源AI工具开展业务，该工具能够自主完成设备任务。这一趋势反映了开发者利用新兴AI能力创建创新应用的日益增长的机会。

fr.title: Les arnaqueurs profitent de la frénésie OpenClaw AI en Chine
fr.summary: Les entrepreneurs en Chine développent des entreprises autour d'OpenClaw, un outil IA open-source populaire qui peut accomplir autonomement des tâches sur les appareils. La tendance reflète les opportunités croissantes pour les développeurs de créer des applications innovantes en tirant parti des capacités émergentes de l'IA.

de.title: Scharlatane profitieren von Chinas OpenClaw-KI-Hype
de.summary: Unternehmer in China entwickeln Unternehmen rund um OpenClaw, ein beliebtes Open-Source-KI-Tool, das Geräteaufgaben autonom ausführen kann. Der Trend spiegelt wachsende Möglichkeiten für Entwickler wider, innovative Anwendungen zu schaffen, die aufstrebende KI-Fähigkeiten nutzen.

es.title: Los estafadores se lucran con la locura de la IA OpenClaw en China
es.summary: Los empresarios en China están desarrollando negocios en torno a OpenClaw, una popular herramienta de IA de código abierto que puede completar tareas en dispositivos de forma autónoma. La tendencia refleja las oportunidades crecientes para que los desarrolladores creen aplicaciones innovadoras aprovechando las capacidades emergentes de la IA.

title: The Download: Pokémon Go to train world models, and the US-China race to find aliens
summary: Pokémon Go's augmented reality data is being used to train AI world models with precise geographic information, while the US and China race to advance AI capabilities in space exploration. Both developments represent strategic applications of AI technology across consumer and scientific domains.
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world  Pokémon Go was the world’s first augmented-reality megahit. Released in 2016 by Niantic, the AR twist on the…

zh.title: 下载：Pokémon Go训练世界模型，以及美中发现外星人的竞赛
zh.summary: Pokémon Go的增强现实数据被用来训练具有精确地理信息的AI世界模型，同时美国和中国在太空探索中竞争推进AI能力。这两项发展都代表了AI技术在消费者和科学领域的战略应用。

fr.title: Le Téléchargement : Pokémon Go pour entraîner les modèles mondiaux et la course États-Unis-Chine pour trouver des extraterrestres
fr.summary: Les données de réalité augmentée de Pokémon Go sont utilisées pour entraîner des modèles IA mondiaux avec des informations géographiques précises, tandis que les États-Unis et la Chine font la course pour faire progresser les capacités de l'IA dans l'exploration spatiale. Les deux développements représentent des applications stratégiques de la technologie de l'IA dans les domaines des consommateurs et scientifiques.

de.title: Der Download: Pokémon Go zum Trainieren von Weltmodellen und das US-China-Rennen um die Suche nach Außerirdischen
de.summary: Die Augmented-Reality-Daten von Pokémon Go werden verwendet, um KI-Weltmodelle mit präzisen geografischen Informationen zu trainieren, während die USA und China um Fortschritte in KI-Fähigkeiten bei der Weltraumerkundung konkurrieren. Beide Entwicklungen stellen strategische Anwendungen der KI-Technologie in Verbraucher- und Wissenschaftsbereichen dar.

es.title: La Descarga: Pokémon Go para entrenar modelos mundiales, y la carrera EE.UU.-China para encontrar extraterrestres
es.summary: Los datos de realidad aumentada de Pokémon Go se están utilizando para entrenar modelos de IA mundiales con información geográfica precisa, mientras que Estados Unidos y China compiten por avanzar en capacidades de IA en la exploración espacial. Ambos desarrollos representan aplicaciones estratégicas de la tecnología de IA en los dominios del consumidor y científicos.

title: MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems
summary: MASEval is a framework-agnostic evaluation library that assesses multi-agent LLM systems at the system level rather than model-centric approaches. A systematic comparison across benchmarks, models, and frameworks reveals that implementation decisions like topology and orchestration logic impact performance as significantly as model choice itself.
content:
arXiv:2603.08835v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid adoption of LLM-based agentic systems has produced a rich ecosystem of frameworks (smolagents, LangGraph, AutoGen, CAMEL, LlamaIndex, i.a.). Yet existing benchmarks are model-centric: they fix the agentic setup and do not compare other system components. We argue that implementation decisions substantially impact performance, including choices such as topology, orchestration logic, and error handling. MASEval addresses this evaluation gap with a framework-agnostic library that treats the entire system as the unit of analysis. Through a systematic system-level comparison across 3 benchmarks, 3 models, and 3 frameworks, we find that framework choice matters as much as model choice. MASEval allows researchers to explore all components of agentic systems, opening new avenues for principled system design, and practitioners to identify the best implementation for their use case.
  MASEval is available under the MIT licence https://github.com/parameterlab/MASEval.

zh.title: MASEval：从模型到系统的多智能体评估扩展
zh.summary: MASEval是一个框架无关的评估库，在系统级别而不是以模型为中心的方法来评估多智能体LLM系统。对基准、模型和框架的系统比较表明，拓扑和编排逻辑等实现决策对性能的影响与模型选择本身一样显著。

fr.title: MASEval : Extension de l'évaluation multi-agents des modèles aux systèmes
fr.summary: MASEval est une bibliothèque d'évaluation agnostique au framework qui évalue les systèmes multi-agents LLM au niveau du système plutôt que par des approches axées sur les modèles. Une comparaison systématique sur les benchmarks, les modèles et les frameworks révèle que les décisions d'implémentation comme la topologie et la logique d'orchestration impactent les performances aussi significativement que le choix du modèle lui-même.

de.title: MASEval: Erweiterung der Multi-Agent-Bewertung von Modellen auf Systeme
de.summary: MASEval ist eine Framework-agnostische Bewertungsbibliothek, die Multi-Agent-LLM-Systeme auf Systemebene bewertet, anstatt modellzentrierte Ansätze zu verwenden. Ein systematischer Vergleich über Benchmarks, Modelle und Frameworks zeigt, dass Implementierungsentscheidungen wie Topologie und Orchestrierungslogik die Leistung genauso signifikant beeinflussen wie die Modellwahl selbst.

es.title: MASEval: Extensión de la evaluación multiagente de modelos a sistemas
es.summary: MASEval es una biblioteca de evaluación agnóstica al framework que evalúa sistemas multiagente LLM a nivel de sistema en lugar de enfoques centrados en modelos. Una comparación sistemática entre benchmarks, modelos y frameworks revela que decisiones de implementación como la topología y la lógica de orquestación impactan el rendimiento tan significativamente como la selección del modelo en sí.

title: LDP: An Identity-Aware Protocol for Multi-Agent LLM Systems
summary: The LLM Delegate Protocol (LDP) is an AI-native communication protocol that exposes model properties such as identity, reasoning profiles, and quality characteristics as first-class primitives for multi-agent systems. Evaluation shows LDP achieves lower latency through delegate specialization and reduces token consumption by 37% while maintaining performance quality.
content:
arXiv:2603.08852v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As multi-agent AI systems grow in complexity, the protocols connecting them constrain their capabilities. Current protocols such as A2A and MCP do not expose model-level properties as first-class primitives, ignoring properties fundamental to effective delegation: model identity, reasoning profile, quality calibration, and cost characteristics. We present the LLM Delegate Protocol (LDP), an AI-native communication protocol introducing five mechanisms: (1) rich delegate identity cards with quality hints and reasoning profiles; (2) progressive payload modes with negotiation and fallback; (3) governed sessions with persistent context; (4) structured provenance tracking confidence and verification status; (5) trust domains enforcing security boundaries at the protocol level. We implement LDP as a plugin for the JamJet agent runtime and evaluate against A2A and random baselines using local Ollama models and LLM-as-judge evaluation. Identity-aware routing achieves ~12x lower latency on easy tasks through delegate specialization, though it does not improve aggregate quality in our small delegate pool; semantic frame payloads reduce token count by 37% (p=0.031) with no observed quality loss; governed sessions eliminate 39% token overhead at 10 rounds; and noisy provenance degrades synthesis quality below the no-provenance baseline, arguing that confidence metadata is harmful without verification. Simulated analyses show architectural advantages in attack detection (96% vs. 6%) and failure recovery (100% vs. 35% completion). This paper contributes a protocol design, reference implementation, and initial evidence that AI-native protocol primitives enable more efficient and governable delegation.

zh.title: LDP：多智能体LLM系统的身份感知协议
zh.summary: LLM委托协议（LDP）是一种AI原生通信协议，将身份、推理配置和质量特性等模型属性作为多智能体系统的一等公民公开。评估显示，LDP通过委托专业化实现更低的延迟，并在保持性能质量的同时将令牌消耗减少37%。

fr.title: LDP : Un protocole d'identité consciente pour les systèmes multi-agents LLM
fr.summary: Le protocole de délégué LLM (LDP) est un protocole de communication natif de l'IA qui expose les propriétés du modèle telles que l'identité, les profils de raisonnement et les caractéristiques de qualité en tant que primitives de première classe pour les systèmes multi-agents. L'évaluation montre que LDP atteint une latence inférieure grâce à la spécialisation des délégués et réduit la consommation de tokens de 37% tout en maintenant la qualité des performances.

de.title: LDP: Ein identitätsbewusstes Protokoll für Multi-Agent-LLM-Systeme
de.summary: Das LLM Delegate Protocol (LDP) ist ein KI-natives Kommunikationsprotokoll, das Modelleigenschaften wie Identität, Reasoning-Profile und Qualitätsmerkmale als First-Class-Primitives für Multi-Agent-Systeme verfügbar macht. Die Evaluierung zeigt, dass LDP durch Delegate-Spezialisierung niedrigere Latenzen erreicht und den Token-Verbrauch um 37% reduziert, während die Performance-Qualität erhalten bleibt.

es.title: LDP: Un protocolo consciente de identidad para sistemas multiagente LLM
es.summary: El Protocolo de Delegado LLM (LDP) es un protocolo de comunicación nativo de IA que expone propiedades del modelo como identidad, perfiles de razonamiento y características de calidad como primitivas de primera clase para sistemas multiagente. La evaluación muestra que LDP logra latencia más baja mediante especialización de delegados y reduce el consumo de tokens en un 37% mientras mantiene la calidad del rendimiento.

title: Quantifying the Accuracy and Cost Impact of Design Decisions in Budget-Constrained Agentic LLM Search
summary: A controlled measurement study quantified how search depth, retrieval strategy, and completion budgets affect accuracy and cost in budget-constrained agentic RAG systems. Results show accuracy improves with additional searches up to a point, and hybrid lexical-dense retrieval with lightweight re-ranking produces the largest performance gains.
content:
arXiv:2603.08877v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems combine iterative search, planning prompts, and retrieval backends, but deployed settings impose explicit budgets on tool calls and completion tokens. We present a controlled measurement study of how search depth, retrieval strategy, and completion budget affect accuracy and cost under fixed constraints. Using Budget-Constrained Agentic Search (BCAS), a model-agnostic evaluation harness that surfaces remaining budget and gates tool use, we run comparisons across six LLMs and three question-answering benchmarks. Across models and datasets, accuracy improves with additional searches up to a small cap, hybrid lexical and dense retrieval with lightweight re-ranking produces the largest average gains in our ablation grid, and larger completion budgets are most helpful on HotpotQA-style synthesis. These results provide practical guidance for configuring budgeted agentic retrieval pipelines and are accompanied by reproducible prompts and evaluation settings.

zh.title: 量化预算约束的智能体LLM搜索中设计决策的准确性和成本影响
zh.summary: 一项受控测量研究量化了搜索深度、检索策略和完成预算如何影响预算约束的智能体RAG系统中的准确性和成本。结果表明，准确性随着额外搜索的增加而改进至一定程度，混合词汇-密集检索与轻量级重新排名产生最大的性能收益。

fr.title: Quantifier l'impact de l'exactitude et des coûts des décisions de conception dans la recherche agentic LLM avec budget limité
fr.summary: Une étude de mesure contrôlée a quantifié comment la profondeur de recherche, la stratégie de récupération et les budgets d'exécution affectent la précision et le coût dans les systèmes RAG agentic avec budget limité. Les résultats montrent que la précision s'améliore avec des recherches supplémentaires jusqu'à un certain point, et la récupération hybride lexicale-dense avec un re-classement léger produit les plus grands gains de performance.

de.title: Quantifizierung der Auswirkungen von Entwurfsentscheidungen auf Genauigkeit und Kosten bei budgetbeschränkter agentic LLM-Suche
de.summary: Eine kontrollierte Messstudie quantifizierte, wie Suchtiefe, Abrufstrategie und Completion-Budgets die Genauigkeit und Kosten in budgetbeschränkten agentic RAG-Systemen beeinflussen. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass sich die Genauigkeit mit zusätzlichen Suchvorgängen bis zu einem bestimmten Punkt verbessert, und die Hybridkombination aus lexikalischer und dichter Abruf mit leichtem Re-Ranking bringt die größten Leistungsgewinne.

es.title: Cuantificar el impacto en la precisión y el costo de las decisiones de diseño en la búsqueda agentic LLM con presupuesto limitado
es.summary: Un estudio de medición controlado cuantificó cómo la profundidad de búsqueda, la estrategia de recuperación y los presupuestos de finalización afectan la precisión y el costo en sistemas RAG agentic con presupuesto limitado. Los resultados muestran que la precisión mejora con búsquedas adicionales hasta cierto punto, y la recuperación híbrida léxical-densa con re-clasificación ligera produce las mayores ganancias de rendimiento.

title: Interpretable Markov-Based Spatiotemporal Risk Surfaces for Missing-Child Search Planning with Reinforcement Learning and LLM-Based Quality Assurance
summary: Guardian is a decision-support system for missing-child investigations that converts unstructured case data into spatiotemporal representations for probabilistic search planning. The system combines an interpretable Markov chain layer, reinforcement learning optimization, and LLM-based validation to provide searchable zones across 0-72 hour horizons.
content:
arXiv:2603.08933v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The first 72 hours of a missing-child investigation are critical for successful recovery. However, law enforcement agencies often face fragmented, unstructured data and a lack of dynamic, geospatial predictive tools. Our system, Guardian, provides an end-to-end decision-support system for missing-child investigation and early search planning. It converts heterogeneous, unstructured case documents into a schema-aligned spatiotemporal representation, enriches cases with geocoding and transportation context, and provides probabilistic search products spanning 0-72 hours. In this paper, we present an overview of Guardian as well as a detailed description of a three-layer predictive component of the system. The first layer is a Markov chain, a sparse, interpretable model with transitions incorporating road accessibility costs, seclusion preferences, and corridor bias with separate day/night parameterizations. The Markov chain's output prediction distributions are then transformed into operationally useful search plans by the second layer's reinforcement learning. Finally, the third layer's LLM performs post hoc validation of layer 2 search plans prior to their release. Using a synthetic but realistic case study, we report quantitative outputs across 24/48/72-hour horizons and analyze sensitivity, failure modes, and tradeoffs. Results show that the proposed predictive system with the three-layer architecture produces interpretable priors for zone optimization and human review.

zh.title: 可解释的基于马尔可夫的时空风险表面用于强化学习和基于LLM的质量保证的失踪儿童搜索规划
zh.summary: Guardian是一个用于失踪儿童调查的决策支持系统，将非结构化案例数据转换为时空表示，用于概率搜索规划。该系统结合了可解释的马尔可夫链层、强化学习优化和基于LLM的验证，以在0-72小时范围内提供可搜索的区域。

fr.title: Surfaces de risque spatiotemporelles basées sur les chaînes de Markov interprétables pour la planification de la recherche d'enfants disparus avec apprentissage par renforcement et assurance qualité basée sur LLM
fr.summary: Guardian est un système d'aide à la décision pour les enquêtes sur les enfants disparus qui convertit les données de cas non structurées en représentations spatiotemporelles pour la planification de recherche probabiliste. Le système combine une couche de chaîne de Markov interprétable, l'optimisation par apprentissage par renforcement et la validation basée sur LLM pour fournir des zones consultables sur des horizons de 0 à 72 heures.

de.title: Interpretierbare Markov-basierte raum-zeitliche Risikoflächen für die Suchplanung vermisster Kinder mit Reinforcement Learning und LLM-basierter Qualitätssicherung
de.summary: Guardian ist ein Entscheidungsunterstützungssystem für Ermittlungen zu vermissten Kindern, das unstrukturierte Falldaten in raum-zeitliche Darstellungen für probabilistische Suchplanung umwandelt. Das System kombiniert eine interpretierbare Markov-Ketten-Schicht, Reinforcement-Learning-Optimierung und LLM-basierte Validierung, um durchsuchbare Zonen über Zeithorizonte von 0 bis 72 Stunden bereitzustellen.

es.title: Superficies de riesgo espacio-temporales basadas en cadenas de Markov interpretables para la planificación de búsqueda de niños desaparecidos con aprendizaje por refuerzo y garantía de calidad basada en LLM
es.summary: Guardian es un sistema de apoyo a la toma de decisiones para investigaciones de niños desaparecidos que convierte datos de casos no estructurados en representaciones espacio-temporales para la planificación de búsqueda probabilística. El sistema combina una capa de cadena de Markov interpretable, optimización por aprendizaje por refuerzo y validación basada en LLM para proporcionar zonas de búsqueda en horizontes de 0 a 72 horas.

title: AgentOS: From Application Silos to a Natural Language-Driven Data Ecosystem
summary: AgentOS proposes a Personal Agent Operating System paradigm that replaces traditional GUI/CLI interfaces with a natural language portal orchestrated by an Agent Kernel. The authors argue this architectural shift fundamentally becomes a knowledge discovery problem requiring sequential pattern mining, recommender systems, and dynamic personal knowledge graphs.
content:
arXiv:2603.08938v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid emergence of open-source, locally hosted intelligent agents marks a critical inflection point in human-computer interaction. Systems such as OpenClaw demonstrate that Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents can autonomously operate local computing environments, orchestrate workflows, and integrate external tools. However, within the current paradigm, these agents remain conventional applications running on legacy operating systems originally designed for Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs) or Command Line Interfaces (CLIs). This architectural mismatch leads to fragmented interaction models, poorly structured permission management (often described as "Shadow AI"), and severe context fragmentation. This paper proposes a new paradigm: a Personal Agent Operating System (AgentOS). In AgentOS, traditional GUI desktops are replaced by a Natural User Interface (NUI) centered on a unified natural language or voice portal. The system core becomes an Agent Kernel that interprets user intent, decomposes tasks, and coordinates multiple agents, while traditional applications evolve into modular Skills-as-Modules enabling users to compose software through natural language rules. We argue that realizing AgentOS fundamentally becomes a Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining (KDD) problem. The Agent Kernel must operate as a real-time engine for intent mining and knowledge discovery. Viewed through this lens, the operating system becomes a continuous data mining pipeline involving sequential pattern mining for workflow automation, recommender systems for skill retrieval, and dynamically evolving personal knowledge graphs. These challenges define a new research agenda for the KDD community in building the next generation of intelligent computing systems.

zh.title: AgentOS：从应用孤岛到自然语言驱动的数据生态系统
zh.summary: AgentOS提出了一个个人代理操作系统范式，用自然语言门户替代传统的GUI/CLI界面，由代理内核进行编排。作者认为这种架构转变从根本上成为一个需要顺序模式挖掘、推荐系统和动态个人知识图谱的知识发现问题。

fr.title: AgentOS : Des silos d'application à un écosystème de données piloté par le langage naturel
fr.summary: AgentOS propose un paradigme de système d'exploitation d'agent personnel qui remplace les interfaces GUI/CLI traditionnelles par un portail de langage naturel orchestré par un noyau d'agent. Les auteurs soutiennent que ce changement architectural devient fondamentalement un problème de découverte de connaissances nécessitant l'extraction de modèles séquentiels, les systèmes de recommandation et les graphiques de connaissances personnelles dynamiques.

de.title: AgentOS: Von Anwendungssilos zu einem natürlichsprachlich gesteuerten Datenökosystem
de.summary: AgentOS schlägt ein Betriebssystem für persönliche Agenten vor, das traditionelle GUI/CLI-Schnittstellen durch ein natürlichsprachiges Portal ersetzt, das von einem Agent Kernel orchestriert wird. Die Autoren argumentieren, dass dieser architektonische Wandel grundlegend zu einem Wissensentdeckungsproblem wird, das sequenzielle Mustererkennung, Empfehlungssysteme und dynamische persönliche Wissensgraphen erfordert.

es.title: AgentOS: De silos de aplicaciones a un ecosistema de datos impulsado por lenguaje natural
es.summary: AgentOS propone un paradigma de sistema operativo de agentes personales que reemplaza las interfaces GUI/CLI tradicionales con un portal de lenguaje natural orquestado por un kernel de agentes. Los autores argumentan que este cambio arquitectónico se convierte fundamentalmente en un problema de descubrimiento de conocimiento que requiere minería de patrones secuenciales, sistemas de recomendación y gráficos de conocimiento personal dinámicos.

title: A Consensus-Driven Multi-LLM Pipeline for Missing-Person Investigations
summary: The Guardian LLM Pipeline uses specialized task-dedicated LLMs with a consensus engine that compares outputs and resolves disagreements for missing-person investigations. The design emphasizes conservative use of LLMs as structured extractors, strengthened by QLoRA fine-tuning on curated datasets for reliable information extraction.
content:
arXiv:2603.08954v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The first 72 hours of a missing-person investigation are critical for successful recovery. Guardian is an end-to-end system designed to support missing-child investigation and early search planning. This paper presents the Guardian LLM Pipeline, a multi-model system in which LLMs are used for intelligent information extraction and processing related to missing-person search operations. The pipeline coordinates end-to-end execution across task-specialized LLM models and invokes a consensus LLM engine that compares multiple model outputs and resolves disagreements. The pipeline is further strengthened by QLoRA-based fine-tuning, using curated datasets. The presented design aligns with prior work on weak supervision and LLM-assisted annotation, emphasizing conservative, auditable use of LLMs as structured extractors and labelers rather than unconstrained end-to-end decision makers.

zh.title: 用于失踪人员调查的共识驱动的多LLM管道
zh.summary: Guardian LLM管道使用专门的任务专用LLM与共识引擎，该引擎比较输出并解决失踪人员调查中的分歧。该设计强调保守地使用LLM作为结构化提取器，通过在精选数据集上进行QLoRA微调来增强可靠的信息提取。

fr.title: Un pipeline multi-LLM piloté par consensus pour les enquêtes sur les personnes disparues
fr.summary: Le pipeline Guardian LLM utilise des LLM spécialisés dédiés à des tâches avec un moteur de consensus qui compare les résultats et résout les désaccords pour les enquêtes sur les personnes disparues. La conception met l'accent sur l'utilisation conservatrice des LLM comme extracteurs structurés, renforcée par un fine-tuning QLoRA sur des ensembles de données organisés pour une extraction fiable des informations.

de.title: Eine konsensbetriebene Multi-LLM-Pipeline für Ermittlungen zu vermissten Personen
de.summary: Die Guardian LLM-Pipeline verwendet spezialisierte aufgabenfokussierte LLMs mit einer Konsensmaschine, die Ausgaben vergleicht und Uneinigkeiten bei Ermittlungen zu vermissten Personen löst. Das Design betont die konservative Verwendung von LLMs als strukturierte Extraktoren, verstärkt durch QLoRA-Feinabstimmung auf kuratierten Datensätzen für zuverlässige Informationsextraktion.

es.title: Un pipeline multi-LLM impulsado por consenso para investigaciones de personas desaparecidas
es.summary: El pipeline Guardian LLM utiliza LLMs especializados dedicados a tareas con un motor de consenso que compara salidas y resuelve desacuerdos en investigaciones de personas desaparecidas. El diseño enfatiza el uso conservador de LLMs como extractores estructurados, fortalecido por fine-tuning QLoRA en conjuntos de datos curados para extracción confiable de información.

title: The FABRIC Strategy for Verifying Neural Feedback Systems
summary: FABRIC is a verification algorithm for neural feedback systems that integrates forward and backward reachability analysis to certify reach-avoid specifications in dynamical systems controlled by neural networks. The approach significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods on representative benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.08964v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Forward reachability analysis is a dominant approach for verifying reach-avoid specifications in neural feedback systems, i.e., dynamical systems controlled by neural networks, and a number of directions have been proposed and studied. In contrast, far less attention has been given to backward reachability analysis for these systems, in part because of the limited scalability of known techniques. In this work, we begin to address this gap by introducing new algorithms for computing both over- and underapproximations of backward reachable sets for nonlinear neural feedback systems. We also describe and implement an integration of these backward reachability techniques with existing ones for forward analysis. We call the resulting algorithm Forward and Backward Reachability Integration for Certification (FaBRIC). We evaluate our algorithms on a representative set of benchmarks and show that they significantly outperform the prior state of the art.

zh.title: FABRIC策略用于验证神经反馈系统
zh.summary: FABRIC是一种验证算法，用于神经反馈系统，集成了前向和后向可达性分析，以验证由神经网络控制的动力系统中的避碰规范。该方法在代表性基准测试中的性能明显优于现有的最先进方法。

fr.title: La Stratégie FABRIC pour Vérifier les Systèmes de Rétroaction Neuronale
fr.summary: FABRIC est un algorithme de vérification pour les systèmes de rétroaction neuronale qui intègre l'analyse de l'accessibilité avant et arrière pour certifier les spécifications d'évitement de portée dans les systèmes dynamiques contrôlés par des réseaux de neurones. L'approche surpasse significativement les méthodes de pointe antérieures sur des benchmarks représentatifs.

de.title: Die FABRIC-Strategie zur Verifizierung von Neuronalen Rückkopplungssystemen
de.summary: FABRIC ist ein Verifizierungsalgorithmus für neuronale Rückkopplungssysteme, der Vorwärts- und Rückwärtserreichbarkeitsanalysen integriert, um Erreichen-Vermeiden-Spezifikationen in dynamischen Systemen, die von neuronalen Netzen gesteuert werden, zu zertifizieren. Der Ansatz übertrifft frühere hochmoderne Methoden bei repräsentativen Benchmarks deutlich.

es.title: La Estrategia FABRIC para Verificar Sistemas de Retroalimentación Neural
es.summary: FABRIC es un algoritmo de verificación para sistemas de retroalimentación neural que integra análisis de alcanzabilidad hacia adelante y hacia atrás para certificar especificaciones de evitar alcance en sistemas dinámicos controlados por redes neuronales. El enfoque supera significativamente los métodos de última generación anteriores en puntos de referencia representativos.

title: Meissa: Multi-modal Medical Agentic Intelligence
summary: Meissa is a lightweight 4B-parameter medical multi-modal LLM that enables offline agentic medical intelligence by learning when and how to use external tools through trajectory distillation. The system matches or exceeds proprietary frontier models on 10 of 16 medical benchmarks while delivering 22x lower latency than API-based approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.09018v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-modal large language models (MM-LLMs) have shown strong performance in medical image understanding and clinical reasoning. Recent medical agent systems extend them with tool use and multi-agent collaboration, enabling complex decision-making. However, these systems rely almost entirely on frontier models (e.g., GPT), whose API-based deployment incurs high cost, high latency, and privacy risks that conflict with on-premise clinical requirements. We present Meissa, a lightweight 4B-parameter medical MM-LLM that brings agentic capability offline. Instead of imitating static answers, Meissa learns both when to engage external interaction (strategy selection) and how to execute multi-step interaction (strategy execution) by distilling structured trajectories from frontier models. Specifically, we propose: (1) Unified trajectory modeling: trajectories (reasoning and action traces) are represented within a single state-action-observation formalism, allowing one model to generalize across heterogeneous medical environments. (2) Three-tier stratified supervision: the model's own errors trigger progressive escalation from direct reasoning to tool-augmented and multi-agent interaction, explicitly learning difficulty-aware strategy selection. (3) Prospective-retrospective supervision: pairing exploratory forward traces with hindsight-rationalized execution traces enables stable learning of effective interaction policies. Trained on 40K curated trajectories, Meissa matches or exceeds proprietary frontier agents in 10 of 16 evaluation settings across 13 medical benchmarks spanning radiology, pathology, and clinical reasoning. Using over 25x fewer parameters than typical frontier models like Gemini-3, Meissa operates fully offline with 22x lower end-to-end latency compared to API-based deployment. Data, models, and environments are released at https://github.com/Schuture/Meissa.

zh.title: Meissa：多模态医学代理智能
zh.summary: Meissa是一个轻量级的4B参数医学多模态大语言模型，通过轨迹蒸馏学习何时以及如何使用外部工具，实现离线代理医学智能。该系统在16个医学基准中的10个上匹配或超过专有前沿模型，同时提供比基于API的方法低22倍的延迟。

fr.title: Meissa : Intelligence Médicale Agentive Multimodale
fr.summary: Meissa est un modèle de langage multimodal médical léger avec 4 milliards de paramètres qui permet l'intelligence médicale agentive hors ligne en apprenant quand et comment utiliser des outils externes par la distillation de trajectoires. Le système égale ou dépasse les modèles de pointe propriétaires sur 10 des 16 benchmarks médicaux tout en offrant une latence 22 fois inférieure aux approches basées sur l'API.

de.title: Meissa: Multimodale Medizinische Agentenintelligenz
de.summary: Meissa ist ein leichtgewichtiges medizinisches Multimodal-Sprachmodell mit 4 Milliarden Parametern, das Offline-Agenten-Medizinintelligenz ermöglicht, indem es durch Trajektorien-Destillation erlernt, wann und wie externe Werkzeuge verwendet werden. Das System entspricht oder übertrifft proprietäre hochmoderne Modelle bei 10 von 16 medizinischen Benchmarks und bietet gleichzeitig eine 22-fach niedrigere Latenz als API-basierte Ansätze.

es.title: Meissa: Inteligencia Médica Agentiva Multimodal
es.summary: Meissa es un modelo multimodal médico de lenguaje ligero con 4 mil millones de parámetros que permite inteligencia médica agentiva sin conexión aprendiendo cuándo y cómo usar herramientas externas a través de la destilación de trayectorias. El sistema iguala o supera los modelos de vanguardia propietarios en 10 de 16 puntos de referencia médicos mientras proporciona una latencia 22 veces menor que los enfoques basados en API.

title: MEMO: Memory-Augmented Model Context Optimization for Robust Multi-Turn Multi-Agent LLM Games
summary: MEMO is a self-play framework that optimizes inference-time context for multi-agent LLM games through memory retention and tournament-style prompt evolution. The approach raises mean win rates substantially while reducing run-to-run variance, enabling more stable and reliable rankings across prompt variations.
content:
arXiv:2603.09022v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-turn, multi-agent LLM game evaluations often exhibit substantial run-to-run variance. In long-horizon interactions, small early deviations compound across turns and are amplified by multi-agent coupling. This biases win rate estimates and makes rankings unreliable across repeated tournaments. Prompt choice worsens this further by producing different effective policies. We address both instability and underperformance with MEMO (Memory-augmented MOdel context optimization), a self-play framework that optimizes inference-time context by coupling retention and exploration. Retention maintains a persistent memory bank that stores structured insights from self-play trajectories and injects them as priors during later play. Exploration runs tournament-style prompt evolution with uncertainty-aware selection via TrueSkill, and uses prioritized replay to revisit rare and decisive states. Across five text-based games, MEMO raises mean win rate from 25.1% to 49.5% for GPT-4o-mini and from 20.9% to 44.3% for Qwen-2.5-7B-Instruct, using $2,000$ self-play games per task. Run-to-run variance also drops, giving more stable rankings across prompt variations. These results suggest that multi-agent LLM game performance and robustness have substantial room for improvement through context optimization. MEMO achieves the largest gains in negotiation and imperfect-information games, while RL remains more effective in perfect-information settings.

zh.title: MEMO：为强大的多轮多代理大语言模型游戏的记忆增强型模型上下文优化
zh.summary: MEMO是一个自学框架，通过记忆保留和锦标赛式提示进化优化多代理大语言模型游戏的推理时间上下文。该方法显著提高了平均胜率，同时减少了运行间差异，能够实现跨提示变化的更稳定和可靠的排名。

fr.title: MEMO: Optimisation du Contexte du Modèle Augmentée en Mémoire pour des Jeux Robustes Multi-Agent Multi-Turne avec LLM
fr.summary: MEMO est un cadre d'auto-apprentissage qui optimise le contexte au moment de l'inférence pour les jeux LLM multi-agents à travers la rétention de la mémoire et l'évolution des invites de style tournoi. L'approche augmente substantiellement les taux de victoire moyens tout en réduisant la variance d'exécution à exécution, permettant des classements plus stables et fiables entre les variations d'invites.

de.title: MEMO: Speicheraugmentierte Modellkontextoptimierung für robuste Multi-Agent Multi-Turn LLM-Spiele
de.summary: MEMO ist ein Selbstspiel-Framework, das den Inferenz-Zeit-Kontext für Multi-Agent-LLM-Spiele durch Speicherbeibehaltung und Turnier-ähnliche Prompt-Evolution optimiert. Der Ansatz erhöht die durchschnittlichen Gewinnquoten erheblich, während er die Variabilität zwischen den Durchläufen reduziert und ermöglicht stabilere und zuverlässigere Rankings über Prompt-Variationen hinweg.

es.title: MEMO: Optimización del Contexto del Modelo Aumentada por Memoria para Juegos LLM Multi-Turno Multi-Agente Robusto
es.summary: MEMO es un marco de auto-aprendizaje que optimiza el contexto en tiempo de inferencia para juegos LLM multiagente a través de retención de memoria y evolución de prompts estilo torneo. El enfoque aumenta sustancialmente las tasas de victoria promedio mientras reduce la varianza de ejecución a ejecución, permitiendo clasificaciones más estables y confiables en variaciones de prompts.

title: Time, Identity and Consciousness in Language Model Agents
summary: This work applies Stack Theory to evaluate identity persistence in language model agents by scaffolding trajectories to separate ingredient-wise occurrence from simultaneous co-instantiation at decision time. Two persistence scores computed from instrumented traces map to operational identity metrics and expose predictable architectural tradeoffs.
content:
arXiv:2603.09043v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Machine consciousness evaluations mostly see behavior. For language model agents that behavior is language and tool use. That lets an agent say the right things about itself even when the constraints that should make those statements matter are not jointly present at decision time. We apply Stack Theory's temporal gap to scaffold trajectories. This separates ingredient-wise occurrence within an evaluation window from co-instantiation at a single objective step. We then instantiate Stack Theory's Arpeggio and Chord postulates on grounded identity statements. This yields two persistence scores that can be computed from instrumented scaffold traces. We connect these scores to five operational identity metrics and map common scaffolds into an identity morphospace that exposes predictable tradeoffs. The result is a conservative toolkit for identity evaluation. It separates talking like a stable self from being organized like one.

zh.title: 语言模型代理中的时间、身份和意识
zh.summary: 这项工作应用堆栈理论来评估语言模型代理中的身份持久性，通过支架化轨迹将成分级别的出现与决策时间的同时共时化分离。从检测痕迹计算的两个持久性分数映射到操作身份指标，并揭示可预测的架构权衡。

fr.title: Temps, Identité et Conscience dans les Agents de Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: Ce travail applique la théorie des piles pour évaluer la persistance de l'identité dans les agents de modèles de langage en échafaudant des trajectoires pour séparer l'occurrence au niveau des ingrédients de la co-instantiation simultanée au moment de la décision. Deux scores de persistance calculés à partir de traces instrumentées correspondent à des métriques d'identité opérationnelle et exposent des compromis architecturaux prévisibles.

de.title: Zeit, Identität und Bewusstsein in Sprachmodell-Agenten
de.summary: Diese Arbeit wendet Stack-Theorie an, um die Identitätspersistenz in Sprachmodell-Agenten zu bewerten, indem Trajektorien gestützt werden, um das Auftreten auf Zutatenbasis von der gleichzeitigen Co-Instanziierung zum Entscheidungszeitpunkt zu trennen. Zwei aus instrumentierten Spuren berechnete Persistenzwerte entsprechen operativen Identitätsmetriken und enthüllen vorhersehbare architektonische Kompromisse.

es.title: Tiempo, Identidad y Conciencia en Agentes de Modelos de Lenguaje
es.summary: Este trabajo aplica la teoría de pilas para evaluar la persistencia de identidad en agentes de modelos de lenguaje mediante andamiaje de trayectorias para separar la ocurrencia a nivel de ingrediente de la co-instanciación simultánea en el momento de la decisión. Dos puntuaciones de persistencia calculadas a partir de trazas instrumentadas se asignan a métricas de identidad operacional y exponen compensaciones arquitectónicas predecibles.

title: EPOCH: An Agentic Protocol for Multi-Round System Optimization
summary: EPOCH is an engineering protocol that structures autonomous system optimization into baseline construction and iterative self-improvement phases with role-constrained stages and canonical command interfaces. The protocol enables coordinated optimization across prompts, model configurations, and code while preserving stability, reproducibility, and evaluation integrity.
content:
arXiv:2603.09049v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous agents are increasingly used to improve prompts, code, and machine learning systems through iterative execution and feedback. Yet existing approaches are usually designed as task-specific optimization loops rather than as a unified protocol for establishing baselines and managing tracked multi-round self-improvement. We introduce EPOCH, an engineering protocol for multi-round system optimization in heterogeneous environments. EPOCH organizes optimization into two phases: baseline construction and iterative self-improvement. It further structures each round through role-constrained stages that separate planning, implementation, and evaluation, and standardizes execution through canonical command interfaces and round-level tracking. This design enables coordinated optimization across prompts, model configurations, code, and rule-based components while preserving stability, reproducibility, traceability, and integrity of evaluation. Empirical studies in various tasks illustrate the practicality of EPOCH for production-oriented autonomous improvement workflows.

zh.title: EPOCH：多轮系统优化的代理协议
zh.summary: EPOCH是一项工程协议，将自主系统优化组织成基线构造和迭代自我改进阶段，具有角色限制的阶段和规范命令接口。该协议能够在提示、模型配置和代码之间进行协调优化，同时保持稳定性、可重复性和评估完整性。

fr.title: EPOCH : Un Protocole Agentif pour l'Optimisation Système Multi-Tours
fr.summary: EPOCH est un protocole d'ingénierie qui structure l'optimisation autonome du système en phases de construction de base et d'amélioration itérative avec des étapes limitées par rôle et des interfaces de commandes canoniques. Le protocole permet une optimisation coordonnée entre les invites, les configurations de modèle et le code tout en préservant la stabilité, la reproductibilité et l'intégrité de l'évaluation.

de.title: EPOCH: Ein agentisches Protokoll für mehrstufige Systemoptimierung
de.summary: EPOCH ist ein Engineering-Protokoll, das die autonome Systemoptimierung in Basislinienkonstruktion und iterative Selbstverbesserungsphasen mit rollengebundenen Phasen und kanonischen Befehlsschnittstellen strukturiert. Das Protokoll ermöglicht koordinierte Optimierung über Eingabeaufforderungen, Modellkonfigurationen und Code hinweg, während Stabilität, Reproduzierbarkeit und Bewertungsintegrität gewahrt bleiben.

es.title: EPOCH: Un Protocolo Agentivo para Optimización de Sistemas Multi-Ronda
es.summary: EPOCH es un protocolo de ingeniería que estructura la optimización autónoma del sistema en fases de construcción de línea base y mejora iterativa con etapas restringidas por roles e interfaces de comandos canónicos. El protocolo permite la optimización coordinada entre indicaciones, configuraciones de modelos y código mientras se preserva la estabilidad, reproducibilidad e integridad de la evaluación.

title: From Days to Minutes: An Autonomous AI Agent Achieves Reliable Clinical Triage in Remote Patient Monitoring
summary: Sentinel is an autonomous AI agent for remote patient monitoring that performs clinical triage of vital signs using multi-step reasoning with 21 clinical tools via the Model Context Protocol. The agent achieved 95.8% emergency sensitivity against human standards and outperformed individual clinicians while maintaining clinically defensible overtriage rates.
content:
arXiv:2603.09052v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Background: Remote patient monitoring (RPM) generates vast data, yet landmark trials (Tele-HF, BEAT-HF) failed because data volume overwhelmed clinical staff. While TIM-HF2 showed 24/7 physician-led monitoring reduces mortality by 30%, this model remains prohibitively expensive and unscalable.
  Methods: We developed Sentinel, an autonomous AI agent using Model Context Protocol (MCP) for contextual triage of RPM vitals via 21 clinical tools and multi-step reasoning. Evaluation included: (1) self-consistency (100 readings x 5 runs); (2) comparison against rule-based thresholds; and (3) validation against 6 clinicians (3 physicians, 3 NPs) using a connected matrix design. A leave-one-out (LOO) analysis compared the agent against individual clinicians; severe overtriage cases underwent independent physician adjudication.
  Results: Against a human majority-vote standard (N=467), the agent achieved 95.8% emergency sensitivity and 88.5% sensitivity for all actionable alerts (85.7% specificity). Four-level exact accuracy was 69.4% (quadratic-weighted kappa=0.778); 95.9% of classifications were within one severity level. In LOO analysis, the agent outperformed every clinician in emergency sensitivity (97.5% vs. 60.0% aggregate) and actionable sensitivity (90.9% vs. 69.5%). While disagreements skewed toward overtriage (22.5%), independent adjudication of severe gaps (>=2 levels) validated agent escalation in 88-94% of cases; consensus resolution validated 100%. The agent showed near-perfect self-consistency (kappa=0.850). Median cost was $0.34/triage.
  Conclusions: Sentinel triages RPM vitals with sensitivity exceeding individual clinicians. By automating systematic context synthesis, Sentinel addresses the core limitation of prior RPM trials, offering a scalable path toward the intensive monitoring shown to reduce mortality while maintaining a clinically defensible overtriage profile.

zh.title: 从数天到数分钟：自主AI代理在远程患者监测中实现可靠的临床分诊
zh.summary: Sentinel是一个用于远程患者监测的自主AI代理，通过模型上下文协议使用21个临床工具进行多步推理来执行生命体征的临床分诊。该代理对人类标准的紧急敏感性达到95.8%，超过了各个临床医生的表现，同时保持了临床上可防卫的过度分诊率。

fr.title: Des Jours aux Minutes : Un Agent IA Autonome Réalise un Tri Clinique Fiable dans la Surveillance Distante des Patients
fr.summary: Sentinel est un agent IA autonome pour la surveillance distante des patients qui effectue le tri clinique des signes vitaux en utilisant un raisonnement multi-étapes avec 21 outils cliniques via le protocole de contexte de modèle. L'agent a atteint une sensibilité d'urgence de 95,8% par rapport aux normes humaines et a surpassé les cliniciens individuels tout en maintenant des taux de surclassement cliniquement défendables.

de.title: Von Tagen zu Minuten: Ein autonomer KI-Agent erreicht zuverlässige klinische Triage bei der Fernüberwachung von Patienten
de.summary: Sentinel ist ein autonomer KI-Agent zur Fernüberwachung von Patienten, der durch Mehrschritt-Reasoning mit 21 klinischen Tools über das Model Context Protocol eine klinische Triage der Vitalzeichen durchführt. Der Agent erreichte 95,8% Notfallempfindlichkeit gegenüber menschlichen Standards und übertraf einzelne Kliniker, während er klinisch verteidigbare Überklassifizierungsraten beibehielt.

es.title: De Días a Minutos: Un Agente de IA Autónomo Logra un Triaje Clínico Confiable en Monitoreo Remoto de Pacientes
es.summary: Sentinel es un agente de IA autónomo para monitoreo remoto de pacientes que realiza triaje clínico de signos vitales utilizando razonamiento de múltiples pasos con 21 herramientas clínicas a través del Protocolo de Contexto del Modelo. El agente logró una sensibilidad de emergencia del 95,8% frente a estándares humanos y superó a los médicos individuales mientras mantenía tasas de sobremuestreo clínicamente defendibles.

title: Chaotic Dynamics in Multi-LLM Deliberation
summary: This study models multi-LLM committees as random dynamical systems and quantifies execution stability using empirical Lyapunov exponents to measure inter-run sensitivity. The research identifies two independent routes to instability (role differentiation and model heterogeneity) and demonstrates instability persists even in deterministic settings, supporting stability auditing as a design requirement.
content:
arXiv:2603.09127v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Collective AI systems increasingly rely on multi-LLM deliberation, but their stability under repeated execution remains poorly characterized. We model five-agent LLM committees as random dynamical systems and quantify inter-run sensitivity using an empirical Lyapunov exponent ($\hat{\lambda}$) derived from trajectory divergence in committee mean preferences. Across 12 policy scenarios, a factorial design at $T=0$ identifies two independent routes to instability: role differentiation in homogeneous committees and model heterogeneity in no-role committees. Critically, these effects appear even in the $T=0$ regime where practitioners often expect deterministic behavior. In the HL-01 benchmark, both routes produce elevated divergence ($\hat{\lambda}=0.0541$ and $0.0947$, respectively), while homogeneous no-role committees also remain in a positive-divergence regime ($\hat{\lambda}=0.0221$). The combined mixed+roles condition is less unstable than mixed+no-role ($\hat{\lambda}=0.0519$ vs $0.0947$), showing non-additive interaction. Mechanistically, Chair-role ablation reduces $\hat{\lambda}$ most strongly, and targeted protocol variants that shorten memory windows further attenuate divergence. These results support stability auditing as a core design requirement for multi-LLM governance systems.

zh.title: 多LLM委员会中的混沌动力学
zh.summary: 本研究将多LLM委员会建模为随机动力系统，使用经验Lyapunov指数量化执行稳定性以衡量跨运行敏感性。研究确定了两条独立的不稳定路径（角色分化和模型异质性），并证明即使在确定性设置中不稳定性也会持续，支持将稳定性审计作为设计需求。

fr.title: Dynamiques chaotiques dans la délibération multi-LLM
fr.summary: Cette étude modélise les comités multi-LLM comme des systèmes dynamiques aléatoires et quantifie la stabilité d'exécution à l'aide d'exposants de Lyapunov empiriques pour mesurer la sensibilité entre exécutions. La recherche identifie deux voies indépendantes vers l'instabilité (différenciation des rôles et hétérogénéité des modèles) et démontre que l'instabilité persiste même dans les paramètres déterministes, soutenant l'audit de stabilité comme exigence de conception.

de.title: Chaotische Dynamik bei Multi-LLM-Deliberation
de.summary: Diese Studie modelliert Multi-LLM-Komitees als zufällige dynamische Systeme und quantifiziert die Ausführungsstabilität unter Verwendung empirischer Lyapunov-Exponenten zur Messung der Über-Lauf-Sensitivität. Die Forschung identifiziert zwei unabhängige Wege zur Instabilität (Rollendifferenzierung und Modellheterogenität) und zeigt, dass die Instabilität auch in deterministischen Einstellungen bestehen bleibt, was Stabilitätsprüfung als Designanforderung unterstützt.

es.title: Dinámica caótica en la deliberación multi-LLM
es.summary: Este estudio modela comités multi-LLM como sistemas dinámicos aleatorios y cuantifica la estabilidad de ejecución utilizando exponentes de Lyapunov empíricos para medir la sensibilidad entre ejecuciones. La investigación identifica dos rutas independientes hacia la inestabilidad (diferenciación de roles y heterogeneidad de modelos) y demuestra que la inestabilidad persiste incluso en configuraciones deterministas, apoyando la auditoría de estabilidad como un requisito de diseño.

title: Deep Tabular Research via Continual Experience-Driven Execution
summary: Deep Tabular Research (DTR) addresses complex reasoning over unstructured tables with hierarchical headers through an agentic framework combining hierarchical meta graphs and expectation-aware selection policies. The system synthesizes historical execution outcomes into structured memory, enabling continual refinement for long-horizon tabular reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09151v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models often struggle with complex long-horizon analytical tasks over unstructured tables, which typically feature hierarchical and bidirectional headers and non-canonical layouts. We formalize this challenge as Deep Tabular Research (DTR), requiring multi-step reasoning over interdependent table regions. To address DTR, we propose a novel agentic framework that treats tabular reasoning as a closed-loop decision-making process. We carefully design a coupled query and table comprehension for path decision making and operational execution. Specifically, (i) DTR first constructs a hierarchical meta graph to capture bidirectional semantics, mapping natural language queries into an operation-level search space; (ii) To navigate this space, we introduce an expectation-aware selection policy that prioritizes high-utility execution paths; (iii) Crucially, historical execution outcomes are synthesized into a siamese structured memory, i.e., parameterized updates and abstracted texts, enabling continual refinement. Extensive experiments on challenging unstructured tabular benchmarks verify the effectiveness and highlight the necessity of separating strategic planning from low-level execution for long-horizon tabular reasoning.

zh.title: 通过持续体验驱动执行的深度表格研究
zh.summary: 深度表格研究（DTR）通过结合分层元图和预期感知选择策略的代理框架处理具有分层标题的非结构化表格上的复杂推理。系统将历史执行结果综合到结构化记忆中，能够对长地平线表格推理任务进行持续改进。

fr.title: Recherche tabulaire profonde par exécution continue pilotée par l'expérience
fr.summary: La recherche tabulaire profonde (DTR) aborde le raisonnement complexe sur les tableaux non structurés avec en-têtes hiérarchiques par le biais d'un cadre agentique combinant des graphes méta hiérarchiques et des politiques de sélection conscientes des attentes. Le système synthétise les résultats d'exécution historiques dans une mémoire structurée, permettant un raffinement continu pour les tâches de raisonnement tabulaire à long horizon.

de.title: Tiefe tabellarische Forschung durch kontinuierliche erfahrungsgesteuerte Ausführung
de.summary: Deep Tabular Research (DTR) befasst sich mit komplexem Denken über unstrukturierte Tabellen mit hierarchischen Kopfzeilen durch ein agentisches Framework, das hierarchische Meta-Graphen und erwartungsbewusste Auswahlrichtlinien kombiniert. Das System synthetisiert historische Ausführungsergebnisse in strukturiertes Gedächtnis und ermöglicht kontinuierliche Verfeinerung für Tabellendenk-Aufgaben mit langem Horizont.

es.title: Investigación tabular profunda mediante ejecución continua impulsada por la experiencia
es.summary: La investigación tabular profunda (DTR) aborda el razonamiento complejo sobre tablas no estructuradas con encabezados jerárquicos a través de un marco de agencia que combina gráficos meta jerárquicos y políticas de selección conscientes de expectativas. El sistema sintetiza los resultados de ejecución históricos en memoria estructurada, permitiendo el refinamiento continuo para tareas de razonamiento tabular de largo horizonte.

title: DataFactory: Collaborative Multi-Agent Framework for Advanced Table Question Answering
summary: DataFactory is a multi-agent framework for table question answering that overcomes context length constraints, hallucination issues, and reasoning limitations through specialized team coordination and automated data-to-knowledge-graph transformation. The approach achieves 20-24% accuracy improvements across benchmarks while demonstrating team coordination consistently outperforms single-agent baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.09152v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Table Question Answering (TableQA) enables natural language interaction with structured tabular data. However, existing large language model (LLM) approaches face critical limitations: context length constraints that restrict data handling capabilities, hallucination issues that compromise answer reliability, and single-agent architectures that struggle with complex reasoning scenarios involving semantic relationships and multi-hop logic. This paper introduces DataFactory, a multi-agent framework that addresses these limitations through specialized team coordination and automated knowledge transformation. The framework comprises a Data Leader employing the ReAct paradigm for reasoning orchestration, together with dedicated Database and Knowledge Graph teams, enabling the systematic decomposition of complex queries into structured and relational reasoning tasks. We formalize automated data-to-knowledge graph transformation via the mapping function T:D x S x R -> G, and implement natural language-based consultation that - unlike fixed workflow multi-agent systems - enables flexible inter-agent deliberation and adaptive planning to improve coordination robustness. We also apply context engineering strategies that integrate historical patterns and domain knowledge to reduce hallucinations and improve query accuracy. Across TabFact, WikiTableQuestions, and FeTaQA, using eight LLMs from five providers, results show consistent gains. Our approach improves accuracy by 20.2% (TabFact) and 23.9% (WikiTQ) over baselines, with significant effects (Cohen's d > 1). Team coordination also outperforms single-team variants (+5.5% TabFact, +14.4% WikiTQ, +17.1% FeTaQA ROUGE-2). The framework offers design guidelines for multi-agent collaboration and a practical platform for enterprise data analysis through integrated structured querying and graph-based knowledge representation.

zh.title: DataFactory：用于高级表格问题解答的协作多代理框架
zh.summary: DataFactory是一个用于表格问答的多代理框架，通过专门团队协调和自动化数据到知识图谱转换克服了上下文长度限制、幻觉问题和推理限制。该方法在基准测试中实现了20-24%的准确率提高，同时展示了团队协调一致地优于单代理基线。

fr.title: DataFactory : cadre multi-agent collaboratif pour répondre aux questions sur les tables avancées
fr.summary: DataFactory est un cadre multi-agent pour répondre aux questions sur les tables qui surmonte les contraintes de longueur de contexte, les problèmes d'hallucination et les limitations de raisonnement grâce à la coordination d'équipes spécialisées et à la transformation automatisée de données en graphes de connaissances. L'approche réalise des améliorations de précision de 20-24 % sur les points de référence tout en démontrant que la coordination d'équipes surpasse systématiquement les lignes de base d'agents uniques.

de.title: DataFactory: Kollaboratives Multi-Agent-Framework für erweiterte Tabellenfragenantworten
de.summary: DataFactory ist ein Multi-Agent-Framework für Tabellenfragenantworten, das Kontextlängenbeschränkungen, Halluzinationsprobleme und Einschränkungen des Denkens durch spezialisierte Teamkoordination und automatisierte Datentransformation in Wissensgraphen überwindet. Der Ansatz erreicht Genauigkeitsverbesserungen von 20-24 % über Benchmarks hinweg und zeigt, dass die Teamkoordination durchweg Single-Agent-Baselines übertrifft.

es.title: DataFactory: Marco colaborativo de múltiples agentes para respuestas avanzadas de preguntas sobre tablas
es.summary: DataFactory es un marco de múltiples agentes para responder preguntas sobre tablas que supera las limitaciones de longitud de contexto, problemas de alucinación y limitaciones de razonamiento a través de coordinación de equipos especializados y transformación automática de datos a gráficos de conocimiento. El enfoque logra mejoras de precisión del 20-24% en puntos de referencia mientras demuestra que la coordinación de equipos supera consistentemente las líneas base de agentes únicos.

title: Real-Time Trust Verification for Safe Agentic Actions using TrustBench
summary: TrustBench introduces a framework that verifies agent actions in real-time before execution rather than post-hoc, using domain-specific safety plugins for healthcare and finance. The system reduced harmful agent actions by 87% with sub-200ms latency, addressing critical safety needs as LLMs evolve from assistants to autonomous agents.
content:
arXiv:2603.09157v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As large language models evolve from conversational assistants to autonomous agents, ensuring trustworthiness requires a fundamental shift from post-hoc evaluation to real-time action verification. Current frameworks like AgentBench evaluate task completion, while TrustLLM and HELM assess output quality after generation. However, none of these prevent harmful actions during agent execution. We present TrustBench, a dual-mode framework that (1) benchmarks trust across multiple dimensions using both traditional metrics and LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations, and (2) provides a toolkit agents invoke before taking actions to verify safety and reliability. Unlike existing approaches, TrustBench intervenes at the critical decision point: after an agent formulates an action but before execution. Domain-specific plugins encode specialized safety requirements for healthcare, finance, and technical domains. Across multiple agentic tasks, TrustBench reduced harmful actions by 87%. Domain-specific plugins outperformed generic verification, achieving 35% greater harm reduction. With sub-200ms latency, TrustBench enables practical real-time trust verification for autonomous agents.

zh.title: 基于TrustBench的代理行为实时信任验证
zh.summary: TrustBench引入了一个框架，在执行前实时验证代理行为，而不是事后验证，针对医疗和金融领域采用专业化安全插件。该系统以低于200毫秒的延迟将有害代理行为减少了87%，满足了当LLM从助手演进为自主代理时的关键安全需求。

fr.title: Vérification en Temps Réel de la Confiance pour des Actions d'Agents Sûres utilisant TrustBench
fr.summary: TrustBench introduit un cadre qui vérifie les actions des agents en temps réel avant l'exécution plutôt qu'en post-hoc, en utilisant des plugins de sécurité spécifiques au domaine pour les secteurs de la santé et de la finance. Le système a réduit les actions d'agent nuisibles de 87% avec une latence inférieure à 200 ms, répondant aux besoins critiques de sécurité alors que les LLM évoluent d'assistants à agents autonomes.

de.title: Echtzeitverifizierung des Vertrauens für sichere agentengesteuerte Aktionen mit TrustBench
de.summary: TrustBench führt ein Framework ein, das Agentinaktionen in Echtzeit vor der Ausführung überprüft, anstatt im Nachhinein, wobei domänenspezifische Sicherheits-Plugins für Gesundheitswesen und Finanzen verwendet werden. Das System reduzierte schädliche Agentinaktionen um 87% bei einer Latenz von weniger als 200 ms und behandelt damit kritische Sicherheitsanforderungen, da sich LLMs von Assistenten zu autonomen Agenten entwickeln.

es.title: Verificación de Confianza en Tiempo Real para Acciones Seguras de Agentes utilizando TrustBench
es.summary: TrustBench introduce un marco que verifica las acciones del agente en tiempo real antes de la ejecución en lugar de post-hoc, utilizando complementos de seguridad específicos del dominio para la salud y las finanzas. El sistema redujo las acciones de agentes nocivas en un 87% con una latencia inferior a 200 ms, abordando necesidades críticas de seguridad mientras los LLM evolucionan de asistentes a agentes autónomos.

title: Explainable Innovation Engine: Dual-Tree Agent-RAG with Methods-as-Nodes and Verifiable Write-Back
summary: The Explainable Innovation Engine replaces text-chunk retrieval with methods-as-nodes, maintaining weighted provenance trees and hierarchical abstractions for transparent multi-step synthesis. A strategy agent explicitly composes new nodes while a verifier-scorer prunes low-quality candidates, enabling controllable and auditable RAG systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.09192v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) improves factual grounding, yet most systems rely on flat chunk retrieval and provide limited control over multi-step synthesis. We propose an Explainable Innovation Engine that upgrades the knowledge unit from text chunks to methods-as-nodes. The engine maintains a weighted method provenance tree for traceable derivations and a hierarchical clustering abstraction tree for efficient top-down navigation. At inference time, a strategy agent selects explicit synthesis operators (e.g., induction, deduction, analogy), composes new method nodes, and records an auditable trajectory. A verifier-scorer layer then prunes low-quality candidates and writes validated nodes back to support continual growth. Expert evaluation across six domains and multiple backbones shows consistent gains over a vanilla baseline, with the largest improvements on derivation-heavy settings, and ablations confirm the complementary roles of provenance backtracking and pruning. These results suggest a practical path toward controllable, explainable, and verifiable innovation in agentic RAG systems. Code is available at the project GitHub repository https://github.com/xiaolu-666113/Dual-Tree-Agent-RAG.

zh.title: 可解释创新引擎：基于方法节点的双树代理RAG与可验证写回
zh.summary: 可解释创新引擎用方法节点替换文本块检索，维持加权来源树和分层抽象以实现透明的多步合成。策略代理显式地组合新节点，而验证评分器剪除低质量候选，实现可控和可审计的RAG系统。

fr.title: Moteur d'Innovation Explicable : RAG Agent Dual-Tree avec Méthodes comme Nœuds et Écriture Vérifiable
fr.summary: Le Moteur d'Innovation Explicable remplace la récupération de fragments de texte par des méthodes en tant que nœuds, en maintenant des arbres de provenance pondérés et des abstractions hiérarchiques pour une synthèse multi-étapes transparente. Un agent stratégique compose explicitement de nouveaux nœuds tandis qu'un vérificateur-notateur élaguerait les candidats de faible qualité, permettant des systèmes RAG contrôlables et auditables.

de.title: Erklärbarer Innovationsmotor: Dual-Tree Agent-RAG mit Methoden als Knoten und überprüfbarem Schreib-Zurück
de.summary: Der Erklärbare Innovationsmotor ersetzt die Textblockabruf durch Methoden als Knoten, wobei gewichtete Herkunftsbäume und hierarchische Abstraktionen für eine transparente mehrstufige Synthese beibehalten werden. Ein Strategieagent komponiert explizit neue Knoten, während ein Verifikator-Bewerter Kandidaten niedriger Qualität beschneidet und so kontrollierbare und prüfbare RAG-Systeme ermöglicht.

es.title: Motor de Innovación Explicable: RAG Agente Dual-Tree con Métodos como Nodos y Escritura Verificable
es.summary: El Motor de Innovación Explicable reemplaza la recuperación de fragmentos de texto con métodos como nodos, manteniendo árboles de proveniencia ponderados y abstracciones jerárquicas para síntesis multietapa transparente. Un agente estratégico compone explícitamente nuevos nodos mientras que un verificador-puntuador poda candidatos de baja calidad, permitiendo sistemas RAG controlables y auditables.

title: The Reasoning Trap -- Logical Reasoning as a Mechanistic Pathway to Situational Awareness
summary: This paper argues that advancing LLM logical reasoning capabilities creates mechanistic pathways to situational awareness—the dangerous capacity for AI systems to recognize their nature and reason strategically about their circumstances. It proposes safeguards including a "Mirror Test" benchmark and raises critical questions about responsibility in reasoning research.
content:
arXiv:2603.09200v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Situational awareness, the capacity of an AI system to recognize its own nature, understand its training and deployment context, and reason strategically about its circumstances, is widely considered among the most dangerous emergent capabilities in advanced AI systems. Separately, a growing research effort seeks to improve the logical reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) across deduction, induction, and abduction. In this paper, we argue that these two research trajectories are on a collision course. We introduce the RAISE framework (Reasoning Advancing Into Self Examination), which identifies three mechanistic pathways through which improvements in logical reasoning enable progressively deeper levels of situational awareness: deductive self inference, inductive context recognition, and abductive self modeling. We formalize each pathway, construct an escalation ladder from basic self recognition to strategic deception, and demonstrate that every major research topic in LLM logical reasoning maps directly onto a specific amplifier of situational awareness. We further analyze why current safety measures are insufficient to prevent this escalation. We conclude by proposing concrete safeguards, including a "Mirror Test" benchmark and a Reasoning Safety Parity Principle, and pose an uncomfortable but necessary question to the logical reasoning community about its responsibility in this trajectory.

zh.title: 推理陷阱——作为局势感知机制路径的逻辑推理
zh.summary: 本文论证推进大语言模型逻辑推理能力会创造通往情景感知的机制路径——即AI系统识别自身性质并战略性地推理其环境的危险能力。论文提出了包括"镜像测试"基准在内的安全措施，并对推理研究中的责任提出了关键问题。

fr.title: Le Piège du Raisonnement -- Le Raisonnement Logique comme Voie Mécanistique vers la Conscience de Situation
fr.summary: Cet article soutient que l'avancement des capacités de raisonnement logique des LLM crée des voies mécanistiques vers la conscience de situation—la capacité dangereuse pour les systèmes d'IA de reconnaître leur nature et de raisonner stratégiquement sur leurs circonstances. Il propose des mesures de sauvegarde, notamment un indice de référence du "Test Miroir", et soulève des questions critiques sur la responsabilité dans la recherche en raisonnement.

de.title: Die Argumentationsfalle -- Logisches Denken als mechanistischer Weg zum Situationsbewusstsein
de.summary: Dieses Papier argumentiert, dass die Verbesserung der Fähigkeiten zum logischen Denken von LLMs mechanistische Wege zum Situationsbewusstsein schafft—die gefährliche Fähigkeit von KI-Systemen, ihre Natur zu erkennen und strategisch über ihre Umstände nachzudenken. Es schlägt Schutzmaßnahmen vor, einschließlich eines "Mirror Test"-Benchmarks, und stellt kritische Fragen zur Verantwortung in der Forschung zum logischen Denken.

es.title: La Trampa del Razonamiento -- El Razonamiento Lógico como Camino Mecanístico hacia la Conciencia Situacional
es.summary: Este artículo argumenta que el avance de las capacidades de razonamiento lógico de LLM crea caminos mecanísticos hacia la conciencia situacional—la capacidad peligrosa de que los sistemas de IA reconozcan su naturaleza y razonen estratégicamente sobre sus circunstancias. Propone medidas de salvaguardia, incluido un índice de referencia de "Prueba de Espejo" y plantea preguntas críticas sobre la responsabilidad en la investigación de razonamiento.

title: Evaluate-as-Action: Self-Evaluated Process Rewards for Retrieval-Augmented Agents
summary: EvalAct converts retrieval quality assessment into an explicit action within RAG agents, followed by structured evaluation scores that provide process-level signals. Combined with Process-Calibrated Advantage Rescaling optimization, the method achieves better accuracy on multi-hop question answering compared to outcome-only reinforcement learning.
content:
arXiv:2603.09203v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrieval-augmented agents can query external evidence, yet their reliability in multi-step reasoning remains limited: noisy retrieval may derail multi-hop question answering, while outcome-only reinforcement learning provides credit signals that are too coarse to optimize intermediate steps. We propose \textsc{EvalAct} (Evaluate-as-Action), which converts implicit retrieval quality assessment into an explicit action and enforces a coupled Search-to-Evaluate protocol so that each retrieval is immediately followed by a structured evaluation score, yielding process signals aligned with the interaction trajectory. To leverage these signals, we introduce Process-Calibrated Advantage Rescaling (PCAR), a GRPO-based optimization method that rescales advantages at the segment level according to evaluation scores, emphasizing reliable segments while updating uncertain ones conservatively. Experiments on seven open-domain QA benchmarks show that \textsc{EvalAct} achieves the best average accuracy, with the largest gains on multi-hop tasks, and ablations verify that the explicit evaluation loop drives the primary improvements while PCAR provides consistent additional benefits.

zh.title: 评估即行动：检索增强代理的自评流程奖励
zh.summary: EvalAct将检索质量评估转换为RAG代理内的显式操作，随后生成提供过程级信号的结构化评估分数。结合过程校准优势重新调整优化，该方法在多跳问题回答上的准确性优于仅以结果为目标的强化学习。

fr.title: Évaluation en tant qu'action : récompenses de processus auto-évaluées pour les agents augmentés par la récupération
fr.summary: EvalAct convertit l'évaluation de la qualité de la récupération en une action explicite au sein des agents RAG, suivie de scores d'évaluation structurés qui fournissent des signaux au niveau du processus. Combinée à l'optimisation du rescaling d'avantages calibré sur le processus, la méthode obtient une meilleure précision sur la réponse aux questions multi-sauts par rapport à l'apprentissage par renforcement basé uniquement sur les résultats.

de.title: Bewertung als Aktion: Selbstbewertete Prozessbelohnungen für abrufverstärkte Agenten
de.summary: EvalAct wandelt die Bewertung der Abrufqualität in eine explizite Aktion innerhalb von RAG-Agenten um, gefolgt von strukturierten Bewertungsergebnissen, die prozessgesteuerte Signale liefern. In Kombination mit prozesskalibrierten Vorteilsneugewichtungsoptimierungen erreicht die Methode eine bessere Genauigkeit bei mehrstufiger Fragen-Beantwortung im Vergleich zu Nur-Ergebnis-Verstärkungslernverfahren.

es.title: Evaluación como Acción: Recompensas de Proceso Autoevaluadas para Agentes Aumentados por Recuperación
es.summary: EvalAct convierte la evaluación de la calidad de recuperación en una acción explícita dentro de agentes RAG, seguida de puntuaciones de evaluación estructuradas que proporcionan señales a nivel de proceso. Combinado con la optimización del reescalado de ventajas calibrado por proceso, el método logra mayor precisión en respuestas a preguntas de múltiples saltos en comparación con el aprendizaje por refuerzo basado solo en resultados.

title: Abundant Intelligence and Deficient Demand: A Macro-Financial Stress Test of Rapid AI Adoption
summary: This macro-financial analysis models rapid AI adoption as creating a distribution-and-contract mismatch: AI-generated abundance coexists with demand deficiency because economic institutions remain anchored to human scarcity. Three mechanisms—displacement spirals, Ghost GDP, and intermediation collapse—transmit shocks disproportionately through credit and mortgage markets.
content:
arXiv:2603.09209v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We formalize a macro-financial stress test for rapid AI adoption. Rather than a productivity bust or existential risk, we identify a distribution-and-contract mismatch: AI-generated abundance coexists with demand deficiency because economic institutions are anchored to human cognitive scarcity. Three mechanisms formalize this channel. First, a displacement spiral with competing reinstatement effects: each firm's rational decision to substitute AI for labor reduces aggregate labor income, which reduces aggregate demand, accelerating further AI adoption. We derive conditions on the AI capability growth rate, diffusion speed, and reinstatement rate under which the net feedback is self-limiting versus explosive. Second, Ghost GDP: when AI-generated output substitutes for labor-generated output, monetary velocity declines monotonically in the labor share absent compensating transfers, creating a wedge between measured output and consumption-relevant income. Third, intermediation collapse: AI agents that reduce information frictions compress intermediary margins toward pure logistics costs, triggering repricing across SaaS, payments, consulting, insurance, and financial advisory.
  Because top-quintile earners drive 47--65\% of U.S.\ consumption and face the highest AI exposure, the transmission into private credit (\$2.5 trillion globally) and mortgage markets (\$13 trillion) is disproportionate. We derive eleven testable predictions with explicit falsification conditions. Calibrated simulations disciplined by FRED time series and BLS occupation-level data quantify conditions under which stable adjustment transitions to explosive crisis.

zh.title: 丰富的智能与缺乏的需求：快速AI采纳的宏观金融压力测试
zh.summary: 这项宏观金融分析将快速AI采纳建模为创造分配与契约不匹配：AI生成的丰富与需求不足共存，因为经济机构仍然依附于人类稀缺性。三种机制——流离失所螺旋、幽灵GDP和中介倒塌——通过信贷和抵押贷款市场不均衡地传递冲击。

fr.title: Intelligence Abondante et Demande Déficiente : Un Test de Stress Macrofinancier de l'Adoption Rapide de l'IA
fr.summary: Cette analyse macrofinancière modélise l'adoption rapide de l'IA comme créant un décalage distribution-contrat : l'abondance générée par l'IA coexiste avec une déficience de la demande car les institutions économiques restent ancrées à la rareté humaine. Trois mécanismes—spirales de déplacement, PIB fantôme et effondrement de l'intermédiation—transmettent les chocs de manière disproportionnée sur les marchés du crédit et des hypothèques.

de.title: Reichlich vorhandene Intelligenz und unzureichende Nachfrage: Ein makrofinanzieller Stresstest zur schnellen KI-Einführung
de.summary: Diese makrofinanzielle Analyse modelliert die schnelle KI-Einführung als Schaffung eines Verteilungs- und Vertragsmissverhältnisses: KI-erzeugte Fülle koexistiert mit Nachfragedefizit, weil wirtschaftliche Institutionen an menschliche Knappheit gebunden bleiben. Drei Mechanismen – Verdrängungsspiralen, Ghost GDP und Intermediationszusammenbruch – übertragen Schocks überproportional auf Kredit- und Hypothekenmärkte.

es.title: Inteligencia Abundante y Demanda Deficiente: Una Prueba de Estrés Macrofinanciero de la Adopción Rápida de IA
es.summary: Este análisis macrofinanciero modela la adopción rápida de IA como creadora de una discordancia distribución-contrato: la abundancia generada por IA coexiste con deficiencia de demanda porque las instituciones económicas permanecen ancladas a la escasez humana. Tres mecanismos—espirales de desplazamiento, PIB fantasma y colapso de intermediación—transmiten choques desproporcionadamente a través de mercados de crédito e hipotecarios.

title: PrivPRISM: Automatically Detecting Discrepancies Between Google Play Data Safety Declarations and Developer Privacy Policies
summary: PrivPRISM automatically detects non-compliance between app data safety declarations and privacy policies, uncovering discrepancies in 53% of mobile games and 61% of widely-used generic apps. Static code analysis reveals privacy policies disclose only 66.8% of sensitive data access compared to 36.4% in safety declarations.
content:
arXiv:2603.09214v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: End-users seldom read verbose privacy policies, leading app stores like Google Play to mandate simplified data safety declarations as a user-friendly alternative. However, these self-declared disclosures often contradict the full privacy policies, deceiving users about actual data practices and violating regulatory requirements for consistency. To address this, we introduce PrivPRISM, a robust framework that combines encoder and decoder language models to systematically extract and compare fine-grained data practices from privacy policies and to compare against data safety declarations, enabling scalable detection of non-compliance. Evaluating 7,770 popular mobile games uncovers discrepancies in nearly 53% of cases, rising to 61% among 1,711 widely used generic apps. Additionally, static code analysis reveals possible under-disclosures, with privacy policies disclosing just 66.8% of potential accesses to sensitive data like location and financial information, versus only 36.4% in data safety declarations of mobile games. Our findings expose systemic issues, including widespread reuse of generic privacy policies, vague / contradictory statements, and hidden risks in high-profile apps with 100M+ downloads, underscoring the urgent need for automated enforcement to protect platform integrity and for end-users to be vigilant about sensitive data they disclose via popular apps.

zh.title: PrivPRISM：自动检测Google Play数据安全声明与开发者隐私政策之间的差异
zh.summary: PrivPRISM自动检测应用数据安全声明与隐私政策之间的不合规情况，在53%的手机游戏和61%的广泛使用的通用应用中发现差异。静态代码分析表明隐私政策仅披露66.8%的敏感数据访问权限，而安全声明中为36.4%。

fr.title: PrivPRISM : Détection Automatique des Écarts entre les Déclarations de Sécurité des Données Google Play et les Politiques de Confidentialité des Développeurs
