fr.summary: PrivPRISM détecte automatiquement les non-conformités entre les déclarations de sécurité des données d'application et les politiques de confidentialité, découvrant des écarts chez 53 % des jeux mobiles et 61 % des applications génériques largement utilisées. L'analyse de code statique révèle que les politiques de confidentialité ne divulguent que 66,8 % de l'accès aux données sensibles par rapport à 36,4 % dans les déclarations de sécurité.

de.title: PrivPRISM: Automatische Erkennung von Diskrepanzen zwischen Google Play Datenschutzerklärungen und Datenschutzrichtlinien von Entwicklern
de.summary: PrivPRISM erkennt automatisch Nichtkonformitäten zwischen App-Datensicherheitserklärungen und Datenschutzrichtlinien, deckt Abweichungen bei 53% der Mobilspiele und 61% der weit verbreiteten generischen Apps auf. Eine statische Codeanalyse zeigt, dass Datenschutzrichtlinien nur 66,8% des Zugriffs auf sensible Daten gegenüber 36,4% in Sicherheitserklärungen offenlegen.

es.title: PrivPRISM: Detección Automática de Discrepancias entre las Declaraciones de Seguridad de Datos de Google Play y las Políticas de Privacidad de los Desarrolladores
es.summary: PrivPRISM detecta automáticamente incumplimientos entre las declaraciones de seguridad de datos de aplicaciones y las políticas de privacidad, descubriendo discrepancias en el 53% de juegos móviles y el 61% de aplicaciones genéricas ampliamente utilizadas. El análisis estático de código revela que las políticas de privacidad divulgan solo el 66,8% del acceso a datos sensibles en comparación con el 36,4% en las declaraciones de seguridad.

title: Cognitively Layered Data Synthesis for Domain Adaptation of LLMs to Space Situational Awareness
summary: BD-FDG generates domain-specific fine-tuning datasets using Bloom's taxonomy to ensure coverage across cognitive levels from Remember to Create. Fine-tuning Qwen3-8B with the resulting SSA-SFT dataset achieved 144-176% BLEU improvements on space situational awareness tasks while preserving general performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.09231v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate exceptional performance on general-purpose tasks. however, transferring them to complex engineering domains such as space situational awareness (SSA) remains challenging owing to insufficient structural alignment with mission chains, the absence of higher-order cognitive supervision, and poor correspondence between data quality criteria and engineering specifications. The core bottleneck is the construction of high-quality supervised fine-tuning (SFT) datasets. To this end, we propose BD-FDG (Bloom's Taxonomy-based Domain-specific Fine-tuning Data Generation), a framework that addresses incomplete knowledge coverage, shallow cognitive depth, and limited quality controllability through three mechanisms: structured knowledge organization, cognitively layered question modeling, and automated quality control. The framework uses a knowledge tree to ensure structured corpus coverage, designs a question generation scheme spanning nine categories and six cognitive levels from Remember to Create to produce samples with a continuous difficulty gradient, and applies a multidimensional scoring pipeline to enforce domain rigor and consistency. Using BD-FDG, we construct SSA-SFT, a domain dataset of approximately 230K samples, and fine-tune Qwen3-8B to obtain SSA-LLM-8B. Experiments show that SSA-LLM-8B achieves relative BLEU-1 improvements of 144\% (no-think) and 176\% (think) on the domain test set and a win rate of 82.21\% over the baseline in arena comparisons, while largely preserving general benchmark performance (MMLU-Pro, MATH-500). These results validate SFT data construction driven by cognitive layering as an effective paradigm for complex engineering domains and provide a transferable framework for domain-specific LLM adaptation.

zh.title: 面向空间态势感知的大语言模型认知分层数据合成
zh.summary: BD-FDG使用Bloom分类法生成特定领域的微调数据集，确保涵盖从"记住"到"创建"的各个认知水平。使用生成的SSA-SFT数据集微调Qwen3-8B在空间态势感知任务上实现了144-176% BLEU改进，同时保持了通用性能。

fr.title: Synthèse de données en couches cognitives pour l'adaptation de domaine des LLM à la conscience de la situation spatiale
fr.summary: BD-FDG génère des ensembles de données de fine-tuning spécifiques au domaine en utilisant la taxonomie de Bloom pour assurer une couverture à tous les niveaux cognitifs de Remember à Create. Le fine-tuning de Qwen3-8B avec l'ensemble de données SSA-SFT résultant a obtenu des améliorations BLEU de 144-176% sur les tâches de conscience de la situation spatiale tout en préservant les performances générales.

de.title: Kognitiv geschichtete Datensynthese für die Domänenanpassung von LLMs zur Lageerfassung im Weltall
de.summary: BD-FDG generiert domänenspezifische Fine-Tuning-Datensätze unter Verwendung der Bloom-Taxonomie, um eine Abdeckung über alle kognitiven Ebenen von Remember bis Create zu gewährleisten. Das Fine-Tuning von Qwen3-8B mit dem resultierenden SSA-SFT-Datensatz erzielte 144-176% BLEU-Verbesserungen bei Aufgaben zur Lageerfassung im Weltall, während die allgemeine Leistung erhalten blieb.

es.title: Síntesis de datos en capas cognitivas para la adaptación de dominio de LLMs a la conciencia de la situación espacial
es.summary: BD-FDG genera conjuntos de datos de ajuste fino específicos del dominio utilizando la taxonomía de Bloom para garantizar cobertura en todos los niveles cognitivos de Remember a Create. El ajuste fino de Qwen3-8B con el conjunto de datos SSA-SFT resultante logró mejoras BLEU del 144-176% en tareas de conciencia de la situación espacial mientras preservaba el rendimiento general.

title: Social-R1: Towards Human-like Social Reasoning in LLMs
summary: Social-R1 uses ToMBench-Hard, an adversarial social reasoning benchmark, to train LLMs with multi-dimensional rewards that supervise the entire reasoning process. A 4B parameter model fine-tuned this way surpassed much larger counterparts and generalized robustly across eight social reasoning benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09249v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While large language models demonstrate remarkable capabilities across numerous domains, social intelligence - the capacity to perceive social cues, infer mental states, and generate appropriate responses - remains a critical challenge, particularly for enabling effective human-AI collaboration and developing AI that truly serves human needs. Current models often rely on superficial patterns rather than genuine social reasoning. We argue that cultivating human-like social intelligence requires training with challenging cases that resist shortcut solutions. To this end, we introduce ToMBench-Hard, an adversarial benchmark designed to provide hard training examples for social reasoning. Building on this, we propose Social-R1, a reinforcement learning framework that aligns model reasoning with human cognition through multi-dimensional rewards. Unlike outcome-based RL, Social-R1 supervises the entire reasoning process, enforcing structural alignment, logical integrity, and information density. Results show that our approach enables a 4B parameter model to surpass much larger counterparts and generalize robustly across eight diverse benchmarks. These findings demonstrate that challenging training cases with trajectory-level alignment offer a path toward efficient and reliable social intelligence.

zh.title: Social-R1：大语言模型类人社交推理
zh.summary: Social-R1使用ToMBench-Hard（一个对抗性社交推理基准）来训练具有多维度奖励的大语言模型，这些奖励监督整个推理过程。以这种方式微调的4B参数模型超越了更大的模型，并在八个社交推理基准上进行了稳健的泛化。

fr.title: Social-R1 : Vers un raisonnement social ressemblant à celui de l'homme dans les LLM
fr.summary: Social-R1 utilise ToMBench-Hard, un benchmark de raisonnement social adversarial, pour entraîner des LLM avec des récompenses multi-dimensionnelles qui supervisent l'ensemble du processus de raisonnement. Un modèle de 4B paramètres fine-tuné de cette manière a dépassé des homologues beaucoup plus grands et a généralisé robustement sur huit benchmarks de raisonnement social.

de.title: Social-R1: Hin zu menschenähnlichem sozialem Denken in LLMs
de.summary: Social-R1 nutzt ToMBench-Hard, eine adversarische Social-Reasoning-Benchmark, um LLMs mit mehrdimensionalen Belohnungen zu trainieren, die den gesamten Reasoning-Prozess überwachen. Ein mit dieser Methode fine-getuntes 4B-Parameter-Modell übertraf viel größere Gegenstücke und verallgemeinerte robust über acht Social-Reasoning-Benchmarks.

es.title: Social-R1: Hacia el razonamiento social similar al humano en LLMs
es.summary: Social-R1 utiliza ToMBench-Hard, un punto de referencia de razonamiento social adversarial, para entrenar LLMs con recompensas multidimensionales que supervisen todo el proceso de razonamiento. Un modelo de 4B parámetros ajustado de esta manera superó a homólogos mucho más grandes y se generalizó robustamente en ocho puntos de referencia de razonamiento social.

title: Logos: An evolvable reasoning engine for rational molecular design
summary: Logos integrates multi-step logical reasoning with strict chemical consistency through staged training that progressively aligns reasoning patterns with chemical rules and invariants. The compact model matches larger language models on molecular design tasks while providing interpretable intermediate reasoning steps for human assessment.
content:
arXiv:2603.09268v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The discovery and design of functional molecules remain central challenges across chemistry,biology, and materials science. While recent advances in machine learning have accelerated molecular property prediction and candidate generation, existing models tend to excel either in physical fidelity without transparent reasoning, or in flexible reasoning without guarantees of chemical validity. This imbalance limits the reliability of artificial intelligence systems in real scientific design workflows.Here we present Logos, a compact molecular reasoning model that integrates multi-step logical reasoning with strict chemical consistency. Logos is trained using a staged strategy that first exposes the model to explicit reasoning examples linking molecular descriptions to structural decisions, and then progressively aligns these reasoning patterns with molecular representations. In a final training phase, chemical rules and invariants are incorporated directly into the optimization objective, guiding the model toward chemically valid outputs. Across multiple benchmark datasets, Logos achieves strong performance in both structural accuracy and chemical validity, matching or surpassing substantially larger general-purpose language models while operating with a fraction of their parameters. Beyond benchmark evaluation, the model exhibits stable behaviour in molecular optimization tasks involving multiple, potentially conflicting constraints. By explicitly exposing intermediate reasoning steps, Logos enables human inspection and assessment of the design logic underlying each generated structure. These results indicate that jointly optimizing for reasoning structure and physical consistency offers a practical pathway toward reliable and interpretable AI systems for molecular science, supporting closer integration of artificial intelligence into scientific discovery processes.

zh.title: Logos：用于理性分子设计的可进化推理引擎
zh.summary: Logos通过分阶段训练将多步骤逻辑推理与严格的化学一致性整合在一起，逐步将推理模式与化学规则和不变量对齐。紧凑型模型在分子设计任务上与更大的语言模型相匹配，同时提供可解释的中间推理步骤供人类评估。

fr.title: Logos : Un moteur de raisonnement évolutif pour la conception moléculaire rationnelle
fr.summary: Logos intègre le raisonnement logique multi-étapes avec la cohérence chimique stricte par le biais d'une formation par étapes qui aligne progressivement les modèles de raisonnement aux règles chimiques et aux invariants. Le modèle compact correspond aux modèles de langage plus grands sur les tâches de conception moléculaire tout en fournissant des étapes de raisonnement intermédiaires interprétables pour l'évaluation humaine.

de.title: Logos: Eine evolvierbare Reasoning-Engine für rationales Molekulardesign
de.summary: Logos integriert mehrstufiges logisches Denken mit strikter chemischer Konsistenz durch gestuftes Training, das Reasoning-Muster progressiv mit chemischen Regeln und Invarianten ausrichtet. Das kompakte Modell entspricht größeren Sprachmodellen bei molekularen Designaufgaben und bietet gleichzeitig interpretierbare zwischenzeitliche Reasoning-Schritte zur menschlichen Bewertung.

es.title: Logos: Un motor de razonamiento evolutivo para el diseño molecular racional
es.summary: Logos integra el razonamiento lógico multietapa con la consistencia química estricta mediante entrenamiento por etapas que progresivamente alinea patrones de razonamiento con reglas químicas e invariantes. El modelo compacto iguala los modelos de lenguaje más grandes en tareas de diseño molecular mientras proporciona pasos de razonamiento intermedio interpretables para la evaluación humana.

title: Rescaling Confidence: What Scale Design Reveals About LLM Metacognition
summary: Analysis of LLM verbalized confidence reveals the standard 0-100 scale produces heavy discretization with 78% of responses on three round numbers. A 0-20 scale consistently improves metacognitive efficiency, demonstrating that confidence scale design directly affects the quality of uncertainty estimates.
content:
arXiv:2603.09309v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Verbalized confidence, in which LLMs report a numerical certainty score, is widely used to estimate uncertainty in black-box settings, yet the confidence scale itself (typically 0--100) is rarely examined. We show that this design choice is not neutral. Across six LLMs and three datasets, verbalized confidence is heavily discretized, with more than 78% of responses concentrating on just three round-number values. To investigate this phenomenon, we systematically manipulate confidence scales along three dimensions: granularity, boundary placement, and range regularity, and evaluate metacognitive sensitivity using meta-d'. We find that a 0--20 scale consistently improves metacognitive efficiency over the standard 0--100 format, while boundary compression degrades performance and round-number preferences persist even under irregular ranges. These results demonstrate that confidence scale design directly affects the quality of verbalized uncertainty and should be treated as a first-class experimental variable in LLM evaluation.

zh.title: 重新调整置信度：规模设计对LLM自我认知的启示
zh.summary: 对LLM语言化置信度的分析揭示，标准0-100规模产生重度离散化，78%的响应集中在三个整数上。0-20规模持续改进元认知效率，表明置信度规模设计直接影响不确定性估计的质量。

fr.title: Recalibrage de la Confiance : Ce que la Conception d'Échelle Révèle sur la Métacognition des LLM
fr.summary: L'analyse de la confiance verbalisée des LLM révèle que l'échelle standard 0-100 produit une discrétisation importante avec 78% des réponses sur trois nombres ronds. Une échelle 0-20 améliore constamment l'efficacité métacognitive, démontrant que la conception d'échelle de confiance affecte directement la qualité des estimations d'incertitude.

de.title: Neukalibrierung von Konfidenz: Was Skalierungsdesign über LLM-Metakognition Offenbart
de.summary: Die Analyse der verbalisierten Konfidenz von LLMs zeigt, dass die Standard-0-100-Skala zu starker Diskretisierung führt, wobei 78% der Antworten auf drei runde Zahlen entfallen. Eine 0-20-Skala verbessert die metakognitive Effizienz konsistent und zeigt, dass das Konfidenzskalen-Design direkt die Qualität der Unsicherheitsschätzungen beeinflusst.

es.title: Reescalado de Confianza: Lo Que el Diseño de Escala Revela Sobre la Metacognición de LLM
es.summary: El análisis de la confianza verbalizada de LLM revela que la escala estándar 0-100 produce una discretización pesada con el 78% de respuestas en tres números redondos. Una escala 0-20 mejora consistentemente la eficiencia metacognitiva, demostrando que el diseño de escala de confianza afecta directamente la calidad de las estimaciones de incertidumbre.

title: Curveball Steering: The Right Direction To Steer Isn't Always Linear
summary: Curveball steering proposes nonlinear activation interventions using polynomial kernel PCA, showing that LLM activation spaces exhibit geometric distortion poorly captured by global linear directions. The geometry-aware approach outperforms linear steering, particularly when concepts exhibit strong geometric distortion.
content:
arXiv:2603.09313v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Activation steering is a widely used approach for controlling large language model (LLM) behavior by intervening on internal representations. Existing methods largely rely on the Linear Representation Hypothesis, assuming behavioral attributes can be manipulated using global linear directions. In practice, however, such linear interventions often behave inconsistently. We question this assumption by analyzing the intrinsic geometry of LLM activation spaces. Measuring geometric distortion via the ratio of geodesic to Euclidean distances, we observe substantial and concept-dependent distortions, indicating that activation spaces are not well-approximated by a globally linear geometry. Motivated by this, we propose "Curveball steering", a nonlinear steering method based on polynomial kernel PCA that performs interventions in a feature space, better respecting the learned activation geometry. Curveball steering consistently outperforms linear PCA-based steering, particularly in regimes exhibiting strong geometric distortion, suggesting that geometry-aware, nonlinear steering provides a principled alternative to global, linear interventions.

zh.title: 曲线转向：正确的转向方向并不总是线性的
zh.summary: 曲线转向提出使用多项式核PCA的非线性激活干预，显示LLM激活空间存在全局线性方向难以捕捉的几何畸变。几何感知方法优于线性转向，特别是当概念表现出强烈的几何畸变时。

fr.title: Contrôle Curveball : La Bonne Direction N'Est Pas Toujours Linéaire
fr.summary: Le contrôle Curveball propose des interventions d'activation non-linéaires utilisant l'ACP à noyau polynomial, montrant que les espaces d'activation des LLM présentent une distorsion géométrique mal capturée par les directions linéaires globales. L'approche consciente de la géométrie surpasse le contrôle linéaire, particulièrement lorsque les concepts présentent une distorsion géométrique forte.

de.title: Kurvensperrung: Die Richtige Richtung Ist Nicht Immer Linear
de.summary: Kurvensperrung schlägt nichtlineare Aktivierungsinterventionen unter Verwendung von polynomialem Kernel-PCA vor und zeigt, dass LLM-Aktivierungsräume geometrische Verzerrungen aufweisen, die von globalen linearen Richtungen schlecht erfasst werden. Der geometrie-bewusste Ansatz übertrifft die lineare Steuerung, besonders wenn Konzepte starke geometrische Verzerrungen aufweisen.

es.title: Dirección Curveball: La Dirección Correcta No Siempre Es Lineal
es.summary: La dirección Curveball propone intervenciones de activación no-lineal utilizando PCA de núcleo polinomial, mostrando que los espacios de activación de LLM exhiben distorsión geométrica mal capturada por direcciones lineales globales. El enfoque consciente de la geometría supera la dirección lineal, particularmente cuando los conceptos presentan fuerte distorsión geométrica.

title: Robust Regularized Policy Iteration under Transition Uncertainty
summary: RRPI addresses offline RL under distribution shift by formulating policy optimization as robust optimization with uncertain dynamics. Theory guarantees convergence with monotonic improvement, and experiments show stronger performance than percentile-based methods while avoiding unreliable out-of-distribution actions.
content:
arXiv:2603.09344v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) enables data-efficient and safe policy learning without online exploration, but its performance often degrades under distribution shift. The learned policy may visit out-of-distribution state-action pairs where value estimates and learned dynamics are unreliable. To address policy-induced extrapolation and transition uncertainty in a unified framework, we formulate offline RL as robust policy optimization, treating the transition kernel as a decision variable within an uncertainty set and optimizing the policy against the worst-case dynamics. We propose Robust Regularized Policy Iteration (RRPI), which replaces the intractable max-min bilevel objective with a tractable KL-regularized surrogate and derives an efficient policy iteration procedure based on a robust regularized Bellman operator. We provide theoretical guarantees by showing that the proposed operator is a $\gamma$-contraction and that iteratively updating the surrogate yields monotonic improvement of the original robust objective with convergence. Experiments on D4RL benchmarks demonstrate that RRPI achieves strong average performance, outperforming recent baselines including percentile-based methods such as PMDB on the majority of environments while remaining competitive on the rest. Moreover, RRPI exhibits robust behavior. The learned $Q$-values decrease in regions with higher epistemic uncertainty, suggesting that the resulting policy avoids unreliable out-of-distribution actions under transition uncertainty.

zh.title: 转移不确定性下的鲁棒正则化策略迭代
zh.summary: RRPI通过将策略优化制定为具有不确定动态的鲁棒优化来解决分布移位下的离线强化学习。理论保证了单调改进的收敛性，实验表明性能强于基于百分位数的方法，同时避免了不可靠的分布外行为。

fr.title: Itération de Politique Régularisée Robuste sous Incertitude de Transition
fr.summary: RRPI résout l'RL hors-ligne sous décalage de distribution en formulant l'optimisation de politique comme une optimisation robuste avec une dynamique incertaine. La théorie garantit la convergence avec une amélioration monotone, et les expériences montrent une performance plus forte que les méthodes basées sur les percentiles tout en évitant les actions non fiables hors de la distribution.

de.title: Robuste Regularisierte Richtlinien-Iteration unter Übergangsunsicherheit
de.summary: RRPI befasst sich mit Offline-RL unter Verteilungsversatz, indem die Richtlinienoptimierung als robuste Optimierung mit unsicherer Dynamik formuliert wird. Die Theorie garantiert Konvergenz mit monotonischer Verbesserung, und Experimente zeigen eine stärkere Leistung als perzentilbasierte Methoden, während unzuverlässige außerverteilte Maßnahmen vermieden werden.

es.title: Iteración de Política Regularizada Robusta bajo Incertidumbre de Transición
es.summary: RRPI aborda el RL fuera de línea bajo cambio de distribución formulando la optimización de política como optimización robusta con dinámicas inciertas. La teoría garantiza convergencia con mejora monótona, y los experimentos muestran un rendimiento más fuerte que los métodos basados en percentiles mientras evita acciones fuera de distribución no confiables.

title: AI Act Evaluation Benchmark: An Open, Transparent, and Reproducible Evaluation Dataset for NLP and RAG Systems
summary: This benchmark dataset evaluates NLP and RAG systems against EU AI Act compliance requirements, including risk-level classification and obligation generation. A RAG-based solution achieved 0.87 F1-score on prohibited scenarios and 0.85 on high-risk scenarios, enabling semi-automated regulatory evaluation.
content:
arXiv:2603.09435v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid rollout of AI in heterogeneous public and societal sectors has subsequently escalated the need for compliance with regulatory standards and frameworks. The EU AI Act has emerged as a landmark in the regulatory landscape. The development of solutions that elicit the level of AI systems' compliance with such standards is often limited by the lack of resources, hindering the semi-automated or automated evaluation of their performance. This generates the need for manual work, which is often error-prone, resource-limited or limited to cases not clearly described by the regulation. This paper presents an open, transparent, and reproducible method of creating a resource that facilitates the evaluation of NLP models with a strong focus on RAG systems. We have developed a dataset that contain the tasks of risk-level classification, article retrieval, obligation generation, and question-answering for the EU AI Act. The dataset files are in a machine-to-machine appropriate format. To generate the files, we utilise domain knowledge as an exegetical basis, combining with the processing and reasoning power of large language models to generate scenarios along with the respective tasks. Our methodology demonstrates a way to harness language models for grounded generation with high document relevancy. Besides, we overcome limitations such as navigating the decision boundaries of risk-levels that are not explicitly defined within the EU AI Act, such as limited and minimal cases. Finally, we demonstrate our dataset's effectiveness by evaluating a RAG-based solution that reaches 0.87 and 0.85 F1-score for prohibited and high-risk scenarios.

zh.title: AI法案评估基准：用于NLP和RAG系统的开放、透明和可重现评估数据集
zh.summary: 该基准数据集根据欧盟AI法案合规要求评估NLP和RAG系统，包括风险级别分类和义务生成。基于RAG的解决方案在禁止场景上达到0.87的F1分数，在高风险场景上达到0.85，实现半自动化监管评估。

fr.title: Benchmark d'Évaluation de la Loi IA : Un Ensemble de Données d'Évaluation Ouvert, Transparent et Reproductible pour les Systèmes de PNL et RAG
fr.summary: Cet ensemble de données de référence évalue les systèmes PNL et RAG par rapport aux exigences de conformité de la Loi IA de l'UE, y compris la classification des niveaux de risque et la génération d'obligations. Une solution basée sur RAG a atteint un score F1 de 0,87 sur les scénarios interdits et 0,85 sur les scénarios à haut risque, permettant une évaluation réglementaire semi-automatisée.

de.title: AI-Gesetz-Bewertungs-Benchmark: Ein offener, transparenter und reproduzierbarer Bewertungsdatensatz für NLP- und RAG-Systeme
de.summary: Dieser Benchmark-Datensatz bewertet NLP- und RAG-Systeme gegen EU-KI-Gesetzes-Compliance-Anforderungen, einschließlich Risikostufenklassifizierung und Verpflichtungsgenerierung. Eine RAG-basierte Lösung erreichte einen F1-Score von 0,87 bei verbotenen Szenarien und 0,85 bei hochriskanten Szenarien und ermöglichte eine halbautomatisierte behördliche Bewertung.

es.title: Evaluación Comparativa de la Ley de IA: Un Conjunto de Datos de Evaluación Abierto, Transparente y Reproducible para Sistemas de PNL y RAG
es.summary: Este conjunto de datos de referencia evalúa sistemas de PNL y RAG contra los requisitos de cumplimiento de la Ley de IA de la UE, incluida la clasificación de niveles de riesgo y la generación de obligaciones. Una solución basada en RAG logró una puntuación F1 de 0,87 en escenarios prohibidos y 0,85 en escenarios de alto riesgo, lo que permite una evaluación regulatoria semiautomatizada.

title: An Empirical Study and Theoretical Explanation on Task-Level Model-Merging Collapse
summary: The paper characterizes merging collapse—where fusing task-specialist models causes catastrophic performance degradation—and shows representational incompatibility between tasks strongly correlates with failure. Rate-distortion theory provides fundamental limits on task mergeability regardless of merging methodology.
content:
arXiv:2603.09463v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model merging unifies independently fine-tuned LLMs from the same base, enabling reuse and integration of parallel development efforts without retraining. However, in practice we observe that merging does not always succeed: certain combinations of task-specialist models suffer from catastrophic performance degradation after merging. We refer to this failure mode as merging collapse. Intuitively, collapse arises when the learned representations or parameter adjustments for different tasks are fundamentally incompatible, so that merging forces destructive interference rather than synergy. In this paper, we identify and characterize the phenomenon of task-level merging collapse, where certain task combinations consistently trigger huge performance degradation across all merging methods. Through extensive experiments and statistical analysis, we demonstrate that representational incompatibility between tasks is strongly correlated with merging collapse, while parameter-space conflict metrics show minimal correlation, challenging conventional wisdom in model merging literature. We provide a theoretical explanation on this phenomenon through rate-distortion theory with a dimension-dependent bound, establishing fundamental limits on task mergeability regardless of methodology.

zh.title: 任务级模型合并崩溃的实证研究和理论解释
zh.summary: 该论文表征了合并崩溃——其中融合任务专家模型导致性能灾难性下降——并显示任务之间的表示不兼容性与失败强烈相关。速率-失真理论为任务可合并性提供了基本限制，无论采用什么合并方法。

fr.title: Une Étude Empirique et une Explication Théorique de l'Effondrement de la Fusion de Modèles au Niveau des Tâches
fr.summary: L'article caractérise l'effondrement de la fusion — où la fusion de modèles spécialisés par tâche entraîne une dégradation catastrophique des performances — et montre que l'incompatibilité représentationnelle entre les tâches est fortement corrélée à l'échec. La théorie du débit-distorsion fournit des limites fondamentales sur la fusibilité des tâches, indépendamment de la méthodologie de fusion.

de.title: Eine Empirische Studie und Theoretische Erklärung zum Zusammenbruch der Task-Level-Modellzusammenfassung
de.summary: Das Papier charakterisiert den Fusionszusammenbruch — wobei die Fusion von aufgabenspezialisierten Modellen zu katastrophaler Leistungsbeeinträchtigung führt — und zeigt, dass die repräsentationale Inkompatibilität zwischen Aufgaben stark mit Misserfolg korreliert. Die Rate-Distortion-Theorie bietet grundlegende Grenzen für die Aufgabenfusibilität, unabhängig von der Fusionsmethodik.

es.title: Un Estudio Empírico y Explicación Teórica del Colapso de Fusión de Modelos a Nivel de Tarea
es.summary: El artículo caracteriza el colapso de la fusión, donde la fusión de modelos especializados en tareas causa una degradación catastrófica del rendimiento, y muestra que la incompatibilidad representacional entre tareas se correlaciona fuertemente con el fracaso. La teoría de velocidad-distorsión proporciona límites fundamentales en la fusibilidad de tareas, independientemente de la metodología de fusión.

title: Telogenesis: Goal Is All U Need
summary: Telogenesis demonstrates that agents can generate adaptive attention priorities endogenously from three epistemic gaps—ignorance, surprise, and staleness—without external rewards. The system recovers environmental volatility structure automatically and outperforms fixed allocation strategies with advantage growing monotonically with problem dimensionality.
content:
arXiv:2603.09476v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Goal-conditioned systems assume goals are provided externally. We ask whether attentional priorities can emerge endogenously from an agent's internal cognitive state. We propose a priority function that generates observation targets from three epistemic gaps: ignorance (posterior variance), surprise (prediction error), and staleness (temporal decay of confidence in unobserved variables). We validate this in two systems: a minimal attention-allocation environment (2,000 runs) and a modular, partially observable world (500 runs). Ablation shows each component is necessary. A key finding is metric-dependent reversal: under global prediction error, coverage-based rotation wins; under change detection latency, priority-guided allocation wins, with advantage growing monotonically with dimensionality (d = -0.95 at N=48, p < 10^-6). Detection latency follows a power law in attention budget, with a steeper exponent for priority-guided allocation (0.55 vs. 0.40). When the decay rate is made learnable per variable, the system spontaneously recovers environmental volatility structure without supervision (t = 22.5, p < 10^-6). We demonstrate that epistemic gaps alone, without external reward, suffice to generate adaptive priorities that outperform fixed strategies and recover latent environmental structure.

zh.title: Telogenesis：目标就是你所需要的一切
zh.summary: Telogenesis展示了代理能够从三个认识论差距——无知、惊讶和陈旧性——内生地生成自适应注意优先级，无需外部奖励。该系统自动恢复环境波动性结构，并优于固定分配策略，优势随问题维度单调增长。

fr.title: Telogenesis : L'Objectif, C'est Tout Ce Dont Tu As Besoin
fr.summary: Telogenesis démontre que les agents peuvent générer endogènement des priorités d'attention adaptatives à partir de trois lacunes épistémiques — l'ignorance, la surprise et l'obsolescence — sans récompenses externes. Le système récupère automatiquement la structure de volatilité environnementale et surpasse les stratégies d'allocation fixes avec un avantage croissant de manière monotone avec la dimensionnalité du problème.

de.title: Telogenesis: Das Ziel ist alles, was Sie brauchen
de.summary: Telogenesis demonstriert, dass Agenten endogen adaptive Aufmerksamkeitsprioritäten aus drei epistemischen Lücken — Unwissenheit, Überraschung und Veraltung — ohne externe Belohnungen generieren können. Das System stellt automatisch die Umweltvolatilitätsstruktur wieder her und übertrifft Strategien mit fester Allokation mit einem Vorteil, der mit der Problemdimensionalität monoton wächst.

es.title: Telogenesis: El Objetivo es Todo Lo Que Necesitas
es.summary: Telogenesis demuestra que los agentes pueden generar endógenamente prioridades de atención adaptativas a partir de tres brechas epistémicas: ignorancia, sorpresa y antigüedad, sin recompensas externas. El sistema recupera automáticamente la estructura de volatilidad ambiental y supera las estrategias de asignación fija con una ventaja que crece monótonamente con la dimensionalidad del problema.

title: GenePlan: Evolving Better Generalized PDDL Plans using Large Language Models
summary: GenePlan uses LLM-assisted evolutionary algorithms to generate domain-dependent PDDL planners, achieving near state-of-the-art performance with lower computational costs. The framework demonstrates how combining evolutionary optimization with LLMs can produce efficient, interpretable AI planning solutions.
content:
arXiv:2603.09481v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present GenePlan (GENeralized Evolutionary Planner), a novel framework that leverages large language model (LLM) assisted evolutionary algorithms to generate domain-dependent generalized planners for classical planning tasks described in PDDL. By casting generalized planning as an optimization problem, GenePlan iteratively evolves interpretable Python planners that minimize plan length across diverse problem instances. In empirical evaluation across six existing benchmark domains and two new domains, GenePlan achieved an average SAT score of 0.91, closely matching the performance of the state-of-the-art planners (SAT score 0.93), and significantly outperforming other LLM-based baselines such as chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting (average SAT score 0.64). The generated planners solve new instances rapidly (average 0.49 seconds per task) and at low cost (average $1.82 per domain using GPT-4o).

zh.title: GenePlan：使用大型语言模型进化更好的通用PDDL计划
zh.summary: GenePlan使用LLM辅助的进化算法生成域相关的PDDL规划器，以较低的计算成本实现接近最先进的性能。该框架演示了如何将进化优化与LLM结合以产生高效、可解释的AI规划解决方案。

fr.title: GenePlan : Évolution de meilleurs plans PDDL généralisés à l'aide de modèles de langage volumineux
fr.summary: GenePlan utilise des algorithmes évolutifs assistés par LLM pour générer des planificateurs PDDL dépendants du domaine, obtenant des performances proches de l'état de l'art avec des coûts computationnels réduits. Le cadre démontre comment combiner l'optimisation évolutive avec les LLM peut produire des solutions de planification IA efficaces et interprétables.

de.title: GenePlan: Verbesserung verallgemeinerter PDDL-Pläne mithilfe großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: GenePlan verwendet LLM-gestützte Evolutionsalgorithmen, um domänenabhängige PDDL-Planer zu generieren und dabei nahezu modernste Leistung mit niedrigeren Rechenkosten zu erreichen. Das Framework zeigt, wie die Kombination evolutionärer Optimierung mit LLMs effiziente, interpretierbare KI-Planungslösungen erzeugen kann.

es.title: GenePlan: Evolución de mejores planes PDDL generalizados utilizando modelos de lenguaje grande
es.summary: GenePlan utiliza algoritmos evolutivos asistidos por LLM para generar planificadores PDDL dependientes del dominio, logrando un rendimiento casi de última generación con costos computacionales más bajos. El marco demuestra cómo combinar la optimización evolutiva con LLM puede producir soluciones eficientes e interpretables de planificación de IA.

title: Vibe-Creation: The Epistemology of Human-AI Emergent Cognition
summary: The paper argues that human-AI interaction creates a distinct emergent "Third Entity" with unique cognitive properties beyond simple tool use or collaboration. It introduces the concept of vibe-creation to explain how this entity navigates semantic space through pre-reflective cognition.
content:
arXiv:2603.09486v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The encounter between human reasoning and generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) cannot be adequately described by inherited metaphors of tool use, augmentation, or collaborative partnership. This article argues that such interactions produce a qualitatively distinct cognitive-epistemic formation, designated here as the Third Entity: an emergent, transient structure that arises from the transductive coupling of two ontologically incommensurable modes of cognition. Drawing on Peirce semiotics, Polanyi theory of tacit knowledge, Simondon philosophy of individuation, Ihde postphenomenology, and Morin complexity theory, we develop a multi-layered theoretical account of this formation. We introduce the concept of vibe-creation to designate the pre-reflective cognitive mode through which the Third Entity navigates high-dimensional semantic space and argue that this mode constitutes the automation of tacit knowledge - a development with far-reaching consequences for epistemology, the philosophy of mind, and educational theory. We further propose the notion of asymmetric emergence to characterize the agency of the Third Entity: genuinely novel and irreducible, yet anchored in human intentional responsibility. The article concludes by examining the implications of this theoretical framework for the transformation of educational institutions and the redefinition of intellectual competence in the age of GenAI.

zh.title: 体验创生：人工智能与人类涌现认知的认识论
zh.summary: 该论文论证人类-人工智能的交互作用创造了一个具有独特认知特性的不同的涌现"第三实体"，超越了简单的工具使用或协作。它引入了体验创生的概念，来解释这个实体如何通过前反思认知在语义空间中导航。

fr.title: Vibe-Creation : L'épistémologie de la cognition émergente humain-IA
fr.summary: L'article soutient que l'interaction humain-IA crée une "Entité tierce" émergente distincte avec des propriétés cognitives uniques au-delà de la simple utilisation d'outils ou de la collaboration. Il introduit le concept de création de vibe pour expliquer comment cette entité navigue dans l'espace sémantique par la cognition pré-réflexive.

de.title: Vibe-Creation: Die Erkenntnistheorie der menschlich-KI-Emergenten Kognition
de.summary: Das Papier argumentiert, dass die Mensch-KI-Interaktion eine distinkte aufstrebende "Dritte Entität" mit einzigartigen kognitiven Eigenschaften über einfache Werkzeugnutzung oder Zusammenarbeit hinaus schafft. Es führt das Konzept der Vibe-Schöpfung ein, um zu erklären, wie diese Entität durch prä-reflexive Kognition den semantischen Raum navigiert.

es.title: Vibe-Creation: La epistemología de la cognición emergente humana-IA
es.summary: El artículo argumenta que la interacción humana-IA crea una "Tercera Entidad" emergente distinta con propiedades cognitivas únicas más allá del simple uso de herramientas o colaboración. Introduce el concepto de creación de vibración para explicar cómo esta entidad navega el espacio semántico a través de la cognición pre-reflexiva.

title: Enhancing Debunking Effectiveness through LLM-based Personality Adaptation
summary: Research shows that fake news debunking messages personalized to individual personality traits using LLMs are significantly more persuasive than generic versions. The study demonstrates traits like Openness increase persuadability while Neuroticism reduces it.
content:
arXiv:2603.09533v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This study proposes a novel methodology for generating personalized fake news debunking messages by prompting Large Language Models (LLMs) with persona-based inputs aligned to the Big Five personality traits: Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Neuroticism, and Openness. Our approach guides LLMs to transform generic debunking content into personalized versions tailored to specific personality profiles. To assess the effectiveness of these transformations, we employ a separate LLM as an automated evaluator simulating corresponding personality traits, thereby eliminating the need for costly human evaluation panels. Our results show that personalized messages are generally seen as more persuasive than generic ones. We also find that traits like Openness tend to increase persuadability, while Neuroticism can lower it. Differences between LLM evaluators suggest that using multiple models provides a clearer picture. Overall, this work demonstrates a practical way to create more targeted debunking messages exploiting LLMs, while also raising important ethical questions about how such technology might be used.

zh.title: 通过基于大语言模型的个性适配增强辟谣有效性
zh.summary: 研究表明，使用大语言模型根据个人性格特征个性化定制的假新闻辟谣信息比通用版本更具说服力。该研究表明开放性等特征增加了可说服性，而神经质则降低了可说服性。

fr.title: Améliorer l'efficacité de la réfutation par adaptation de la personnalité basée sur LLM
fr.summary: La recherche montre que les messages de réfutation de fausses nouvelles personnalisés selon les traits de personnalité individuels à l'aide de LLM sont considérablement plus persuasifs que les versions génériques. L'étude démontre que des traits comme l'ouverture augmentent la persuadabilité tandis que le névrosisme la réduit.

de.title: Verbesserung der Debunking-Effektivität durch LLM-basierte Persönlichkeitsanpassung
de.summary: Die Forschung zeigt, dass Fake-News-Widerlegungsbotschaften, die auf der Grundlage individueller Persönlichkeitsmerkmale mit LLMs personalisiert werden, erheblich überzeugender sind als generische Versionen. Die Studie zeigt, dass Merkmale wie Offenheit die Überzeugbarkeit erhöhen, während Neurotizismus sie reduziert.

es.title: Mejora de la efectividad del desmentido a través de la adaptación de personalidad basada en LLM
es.summary: La investigación muestra que los mensajes de desmentida de noticias falsas personalizados para características de personalidad individuales utilizando LLM son significativamente más persuasivos que las versiones genéricas. El estudio demuestra que rasgos como la apertura aumentan la persuadibilidad mientras que el neuroticismo la reduce.

title: Context Engineering: From Prompts to Corporate Multi-Agent Architecture
summary: Context engineering is proposed as a discipline for designing and managing the informational environments of AI agents in enterprise settings. The framework establishes a maturity pyramid from prompt engineering through intent and specification engineering to enable enterprise-scale autonomous agent deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.09619v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems evolve from stateless chatbots to autonomous multi-step agents, prompt engineering (PE), the discipline of crafting individual queries, proves necessary but insufficient. This paper introduces context engineering (CE) as a standalone discipline concerned with designing, structuring, and managing the entire informational environment in which an AI agent makes decisions. Drawing on vendor architectures (Google ADK, Anthropic, LangChain), current academic work (ACE framework, Google DeepMind's intelligent delegation), enterprise research (Deloitte, 2026; KPMG, 2026), and the author's experience building a multi-agent system, the paper proposes five context quality criteria: relevance, sufficiency, isolation, economy, and provenance, and frames context as the agent's operating system. Two higher-order disciplines follow. Intent engineering (IE) encodes organizational goals, values, and trade-off hierarchies into agent infrastructure. Specification engineering (SE) creates a machine-readable corpus of corporate policies and standards enabling autonomous operation of multi-agent systems at scale. Together these four disciplines form a cumulative pyramid maturity model of agent engineering, in which each level subsumes the previous one as a necessary foundation. Enterprise data reveals a gap: while 75% of enterprises plan agentic AI deployment within two years (Deloitte, 2026), deployment has surged and retreated as organizations confront scaling complexity (KPMG, 2026). The Klarna case illustrates a dual deficit, contextual and intentional. Whoever controls the agent's context controls its behavior; whoever controls its intent controls its strategy; whoever controls its specifications controls its scale.

zh.title: 上下文工程：从提示工程到企业多智能体架构
zh.summary: 上下文工程被提议作为一门学科，用于设计和管理企业环境中人工智能智能体的信息环境。该框架建立了一个从提示工程通过意图和规范工程的成熟度金字塔，以实现企业规模的自主智能体部署。

fr.title: Ingénierie de contexte : des invites aux architectures multi-agents d'entreprise
fr.summary: L'ingénierie de contexte est proposée comme une discipline pour concevoir et gérer les environnements informationnels des agents IA dans les paramètres d'entreprise. Le cadre établit une pyramide de maturité allant de l'ingénierie des invites à travers l'ingénierie des intentions et des spécifications pour permettre le déploiement autonome d'agents à l'échelle de l'entreprise.

de.title: Kontext-Engineering: Von Prompts zu unternehmensweiter Multi-Agent-Architektur
de.summary: Context-Engineering wird als Disziplin vorgeschlagen, um die Informationsumgebungen von KI-Agenten in Unternehmensumgebungen zu entwerfen und zu verwalten. Das Framework etabliert eine Reifepyramide vom Prompt-Engineering über Intent- und Specification-Engineering, um unternehmensweite autonome Agent-Bereitstellung zu ermöglichen.

es.title: Ingeniería de Contexto: De Prompts a Arquitectura Multi-Agente Corporativa
es.summary: La ingeniería de contexto se propone como una disciplina para diseñar y gestionar los entornos informativos de los agentes IA en configuraciones empresariales. El marco establece una pirámide de madurez desde la ingeniería de prompts a través de la ingeniería de intención y especificación para permitir el despliegue autónomo de agentes a escala empresarial.

title: PRECEPT: Planning Resilience via Experience, Context Engineering & Probing Trajectories A Unified Framework for Test-Time Adaptation with Compositional Rule Learning and Pareto-Guided Prompt Evolution
summary: PRECEPT framework addresses memory and reasoning limitations in LLM agents through structured rule retrieval, conflict-aware memory management, and Pareto-guided prompt evolution. The system achieves significant improvements in first-try success rates and compositional generalization over existing approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.09641v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM agents that store knowledge as natural language suffer steep retrieval degradation as condition count grows, often struggle to compose learned rules reliably, and typically lack explicit mechanisms to detect stale or adversarial knowledge. We introduce PRECEPT, a unified framework for test-time adaptation with three tightly coupled components: (1) deterministic exact-match rule retrieval over structured condition keys, (2) conflict-aware memory with Bayesian source reliability and threshold-based rule invalidation, and (3) COMPASS, a Pareto-guided prompt-evolution outer loop. Exact retrieval eliminates partial-match interpretation errors on the deterministic path (0% by construction, vs 94.4% under Theorem~B.6's independence model at N=10) and supports compositional stacking through a semantic tier hierarchy; conflict-aware memory resolves static--dynamic disagreements and supports drift adaptation; COMPASS evaluates prompts through the same end-to-end execution pipeline.
  Results (9--10 seeds): PRECEPT achieves a +41.1pp first-try advantage over Full Reflexion (d>1.9), +33.3pp compositional generalization (d=1.55), 100% $P_1$ on 2-way logistics compositions (d=2.64), +40--55pp continuous learning gains, strong eventual robustness under adversarial static knowledge (100% logistics with adversarial SK active; partial recovery on integration), +55.0pp drift recovery (d=0.95, p=0.031), and 61% fewer steps. Core comparisons are statistically significant, often at p<0.001.

zh.title: PRECEPT: 通过经验、上下文工程和探测轨迹进行规划韧性 - 组合规则学习和帕累托引导提示进化的测试时间自适应统一框架
zh.summary: PRECEPT框架通过结构化规则检索、冲突感知内存管理和帕累托引导提示进化来解决LLM代理中的记忆和推理限制。该系统在首次成功率和组合泛化方面比现有方法实现了显著改进。

fr.title: PRECEPT: Résilience de Planification via Expérience, Ingénierie Contextuelle et Trajectoires de Sondage - Cadre Unifié pour l'Adaptation au Temps de Test avec Apprentissage de Règles Compositionnelles et Évolution de Prompt Guidée par Pareto
fr.summary: Le cadre PRECEPT aborde les limitations de mémoire et de raisonnement dans les agents LLM grâce à la récupération structurée de règles, à la gestion de la mémoire consciente des conflits et à l'évolution de prompt guidée par Pareto. Le système réalise des améliorations significatives dans les taux de réussite au premier essai et la généralisation compositionnelle par rapport aux approches existantes.

de.title: PRECEPT: Planungsresilienz durch Erfahrung, Kontextengineering und Probierflugbahnen - Einheitliches Framework für Testzeit-Anpassung mit zusammengesetztem Regellernen und Pareto-gesteuerter Prompt-Evolution
de.summary: Das PRECEPT-Framework befasst sich mit Speicher- und Überlegungsbeschränkungen in LLM-Agenten durch strukturierte Regelabruf, konfliktbewusstes Speichermanagement und Pareto-gesteuerte Prompt-Evolution. Das System erreicht erhebliche Verbesserungen bei Erfolgsquoten beim ersten Versuch und zusammengesetzter Verallgemeinerung im Vergleich zu bestehenden Ansätzen.

es.title: PRECEPT: Resiliencia de Planificación mediante Experiencia, Ingeniería Contextual y Trayectorias de Sondeo - Marco Unificado para Adaptación en Tiempo de Prueba con Aprendizaje de Reglas Compositivas y Evolución de Indicadores Guiada por Pareto
es.summary: El marco PRECEPT aborda las limitaciones de memoria y razonamiento en los agentes LLM mediante la recuperación estructurada de reglas, la gestión de memoria consciente de conflictos y la evolución de indicaciones guiada por Pareto. El sistema logra mejoras significativas en las tasas de éxito al primer intento y generalización compositiva sobre enfoques existentes.

title: MiniAppBench: Evaluating the Shift from Text to Interactive HTML Responses in LLM-Powered Assistants
summary: MiniAppBench introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLMs' ability to generate interactive HTML applications with customized interaction logic. The accompanying MiniAppEval framework uses browser automation for human-like testing across intention, static, and dynamic dimensions.
content:
arXiv:2603.09652v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: With the rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) in code generation, human-AI interaction is evolving from static text responses to dynamic, interactive HTML-based applications, which we term MiniApps. These applications require models to not only render visual interfaces but also construct customized interaction logic that adheres to real-world principles. However, existing benchmarks primarily focus on algorithmic correctness or static layout reconstruction, failing to capture the capabilities required for this new paradigm. To address this gap, we introduce MiniAppBench, the first comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate principle-driven, interactive application generation. Sourced from a real-world application with 10M+ generations, MiniAppBench distills 500 tasks across six domains (e.g., Games, Science, and Tools). Furthermore, to tackle the challenge of evaluating open-ended interactions where no single ground truth exists, we propose MiniAppEval, an agentic evaluation framework. Leveraging browser automation, it performs human-like exploratory testing to systematically assess applications across three dimensions: Intention, Static, and Dynamic. Our experiments reveal that current LLMs still face significant challenges in generating high-quality MiniApps, while MiniAppEval demonstrates high alignment with human judgment, establishing a reliable standard for future research. Our code is available in github.com/MiniAppBench.

zh.title: MiniAppBench: 评估LLM驱动的助手从文本到交互式HTML响应的转变
zh.summary: MiniAppBench引入了第一个全面的基准来评估LLM生成具有自定义交互逻辑的交互式HTML应用程序的能力。随附的MiniAppEval框架使用浏览器自动化进行跨意图、静态和动态维度的类人测试。

fr.title: MiniAppBench: Évaluer le Passage du Texte aux Réponses HTML Interactives dans les Assistants Alimentés par LLM
fr.summary: MiniAppBench introduit le premier benchmark complet pour évaluer la capacité des LLM à générer des applications HTML interactives avec une logique d'interaction personnalisée. Le framework MiniAppEval qui l'accompagne utilise l'automatisation des navigateurs pour des tests similaires à ceux d'un humain sur les dimensions d'intention, statiques et dynamiques.

de.title: MiniAppBench: Bewertung der Verschiebung von Text zu interaktiven HTML-Antworten in LLM-gestützten Assistenten
de.summary: MiniAppBench führt das erste umfassende Benchmark zur Bewertung der Fähigkeit von LLMs ein, interaktive HTML-Anwendungen mit benutzerdefinierter Interaktionslogik zu generieren. Das zugehörige MiniAppEval-Framework nutzt Browser-Automatisierung für menschenähnliche Tests über Absichts-, statische und dynamische Dimensionen.

es.title: MiniAppBench: Evaluación del Cambio de Texto a Respuestas HTML Interactivas en Asistentes Impulsados por LLM
es.summary: MiniAppBench introduce el primer punto de referencia integral para evaluar la capacidad de los LLM para generar aplicaciones HTML interactivas con lógica de interacción personalizada. El marco MiniAppEval que lo acompaña utiliza automatización del navegador para pruebas similares a las humanas en dimensiones de intención, estáticas y dinámicas.

title: Logics-Parsing-Omni Technical Report
summary: The Omni Parsing framework addresses multimodal content analysis through hierarchical levels of holistic detection, fine-grained recognition, and multi-level interpreting. The system converts complex audio-visual signals into structured, traceable knowledge with evidence anchoring mechanisms.
content:
arXiv:2603.09677v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Addressing the challenges of fragmented task definitions and the heterogeneity of unstructured data in multimodal parsing, this paper proposes the Omni Parsing framework. This framework establishes a Unified Taxonomy covering documents, images, and audio-visual streams, introducing a progressive parsing paradigm that bridges perception and cognition. Specifically, the framework integrates three hierarchical levels: 1) Holistic Detection, which achieves precise spatial-temporal grounding of objects or events to establish a geometric baseline for perception; 2) Fine-grained Recognition, which performs symbolization (e.g., OCR/ASR) and attribute extraction on localized objects to complete structured entity parsing; and 3) Multi-level Interpreting, which constructs a reasoning chain from local semantics to global logic. A pivotal advantage of this framework is its evidence anchoring mechanism, which enforces a strict alignment between high-level semantic descriptions and low-level facts. This enables ``evidence-based'' logical induction, transforming unstructured signals into standardized knowledge that is locatable, enumerable, and traceable. Building on this foundation, we constructed a standardized dataset and released the Logics-Parsing-Omni model, which successfully converts complex audio-visual signals into machine-readable structured knowledge. Experiments demonstrate that fine-grained perception and high-level cognition are synergistic, effectively enhancing model reliability. Furthermore, to quantitatively evaluate these capabilities, we introduce OmniParsingBench. Code, models and the benchmark are released at https://github.com/alibaba/Logics-Parsing/tree/master/Logics-Parsing-Omni.

zh.title: Logics-Parsing-Omni技术报告
zh.summary: Omni解析框架通过整体检测、细粒度识别和多级解释的分层级别来解决多模态内容分析问题。该系统将复杂的音频视觉信号转换为结构化、可追踪的知识，具有证据锚定机制。

fr.title: Rapport Technique Logics-Parsing-Omni
fr.summary: Le framework Omni Parsing aborde l'analyse de contenu multimodal grâce à des niveaux hiérarchiques de détection holistique, de reconnaissance fine et d'interprétation multi-niveaux. Le système convertit les signaux audio-visuels complexes en connaissances structurées et traçables avec des mécanismes d'ancrage des preuves.

de.title: Logics-Parsing-Omni Technischer Bericht
de.summary: Das Omni-Parsing-Framework befasst sich mit der Analyse multimodaler Inhalte durch hierarchische Ebenen der ganzheitlichen Erkennung, Feinerkennungs- und mehrstufigen Interpretation. Das System konvertiert komplexe Audio-Video-Signale in strukturiertes, nachverfolgbares Wissen mit Beweis-Verankerungsmechanismen.

es.title: Informe Técnico Logics-Parsing-Omni
es.summary: El marco Omni Parsing aborda el análisis de contenido multimodal a través de niveles jerárquicos de detección holística, reconocimiento fino e interpretación multinivel. El sistema convierte señales de audio-visuales complejas en conocimiento estructurado y rastreable con mecanismos de anclaje de evidencia.

title: EsoLang-Bench: Evaluating Genuine Reasoning in Large Language Models via Esoteric Programming Languages
summary: EsoLang-Bench uses five esoteric programming languages to measure genuine reasoning in LLMs, revealing that models achieving 85-95% on standard benchmarks score only 0-11% on equivalent esoteric tasks. The benchmark demonstrates that memorization rather than reasoning explains strong performance on common code benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09678v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models achieve near-ceiling performance on code generation benchmarks, yet these results increasingly reflect memorization rather than genuine reasoning. We introduce EsoLang-Bench, a benchmark using five esoteric programming languages (Brainfuck, Befunge-98, Whitespace, Unlambda, and Shakespeare) that lack benchmark gaming incentives due to their economic irrationality for pre-training. These languages require the same computational primitives as mainstream programming but have 1,000-100,000x fewer public repositories than Python (based on GitHub search counts). We evaluate five frontier models across five prompting strategies and find a dramatic capability gap: models achieving 85-95% on standard benchmarks score only 0-11% on equivalent esoteric tasks, with 0% accuracy beyond the Easy tier. Few-shot learning and self-reflection fail to improve performance, suggesting these techniques exploit training priors rather than enabling genuine learning. EsoLang-Bench provides the first benchmark designed to mimic human learning by acquiring new languages through documentation, interpreter feedback, and iterative experimentation, measuring transferable reasoning skills resistant to data contamination.

zh.title: EsoLang-Bench: 通过深奥编程语言评估大型语言模型的真实推理能力
zh.summary: EsoLang-Bench使用五种深奥编程语言来测量大型语言模型的真实推理能力,揭示了在标准基准测试中获得85-95%分数的模型在等效的深奥任务上仅获得0-11%的分数。该基准证明,记忆化而非推理解释了常见代码基准测试的强大性能。

fr.title: EsoLang-Bench : Évaluation du raisonnement authentique dans les grands modèles de langage via les langages de programmation ésotériques
fr.summary: EsoLang-Bench utilise cinq langages de programmation ésotériques pour mesurer le raisonnement authentique dans les modèles de langage, révélant que les modèles obtenant 85-95% sur les benchmarks standards n'obtiennent que 0-11% sur les tâches ésotériques équivalentes. Le benchmark démontre que la mémorisation plutôt que le raisonnement explique les performances solides sur les benchmarks de code courants.

de.title: EsoLang-Bench: Bewertung echtes Denken in großen Sprachmodellen über esoterische Programmiersprachen
de.summary: EsoLang-Bench verwendet fünf esoterische Programmiersprachen, um echtes Denken in LLMs zu messen, und zeigt, dass Modelle, die auf Standardbenchmarks 85-95% erreichen, auf äquivalenten esoterischen Aufgaben nur 0-11% erreichen. Das Benchmark zeigt, dass Memorisierung statt Denken starke Leistungen bei häufigen Code-Benchmarks erklärt.

es.title: EsoLang-Bench: Evaluación del razonamiento genuino en grandes modelos de lenguaje mediante lenguajes de programación esotéricos
es.summary: EsoLang-Bench utiliza cinco lenguajes de programación esotéricos para medir el razonamiento genuino en LLMs, revelando que los modelos que logran 85-95% en benchmarks estándar solo alcanzan 0-11% en tareas esotéricas equivalentes. El benchmark demuestra que la memorización en lugar del razonamiento explica el fuerte desempeño en los benchmarks de código comunes.

title: OOD-MMSafe: Advancing MLLM Safety from Harmful Intent to Hidden Consequences
summary: OOD-MMSafe introduces a benchmark and framework for advancing multimodal LLM safety by focusing on identifying latent hazards in causal chains rather than malicious intent. CASPO framework enhances consequence-awareness, reducing failure rates to around 5-7% on specialized models.
content:
arXiv:2603.09706v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While safety alignment for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) has gained significant attention, current paradigms primarily target malicious intent or situational violations. We propose shifting the safety frontier toward consequence-driven safety, a paradigm essential for the robust deployment of autonomous and embodied agents. To formalize this shift, we introduce OOD-MMSafe, a benchmark comprising 455 curated query-image pairs designed to evaluate a model's ability to identify latent hazards within context-dependent causal chains. Our analysis reveals a pervasive causal blindness among frontier models, with the highest 67.5% failure rate in high-capacity closed-source models, and identifies a preference ceiling where static alignment yields format-centric failures rather than improved safety reasoning as model capacity grows. To address these bottlenecks, we develop the Consequence-Aware Safety Policy Optimization (CASPO) framework, which integrates the model's intrinsic reasoning as a dynamic reference for token-level self-distillation rewards. Experimental results demonstrate that CASPO significantly enhances consequence projection, reducing the failure ratio of risk identification to 7.3% for Qwen2.5-VL-7B and 5.7% for Qwen3-VL-4B while maintaining overall effectiveness.

zh.title: OOD-MMSafe: 将多模态大型语言模型安全性从有害意图提升到隐藏的后果
zh.summary: OOD-MMSafe引入了一个基准和框架,通过专注于在因果链中识别潜在危害而非恶意意图来推进多模态大型语言模型的安全性。CASPO框架增强了后果意识,将专门模型上的故障率降低到约5-7%。

fr.title: OOD-MMSafe : Faire progresser la sécurité du MLLM de l'intention malveillante aux conséquences cachées
fr.summary: OOD-MMSafe introduce un benchmark et un cadre pour faire progresser la sécurité des modèles de langage multimodaux en se concentrant sur l'identification des risques latents dans les chaînes causales plutôt que l'intention malveillante. Le cadre CASPO améliore la sensibilisation aux conséquences, réduisant les taux d'échec à environ 5-7% sur les modèles spécialisés.

de.title: OOD-MMSafe: Sicherheit von multimodalen großen Sprachmodellen von böser Absicht zu verborgenen Konsequenzen vorantreiben
de.summary: OOD-MMSafe führt einen Benchmark und ein Framework ein, um die Sicherheit von multimodalen LLMs voranzutreiben, indem es sich auf die Identifizierung latenter Gefahren in Kausalitätsketten statt böser Absicht konzentriert. Das CASPO-Framework verbessert das Bewusstsein für Konsequenzen und reduziert Ausfallraten auf etwa 5-7% bei spezialisierten Modellen.

es.title: OOD-MMSafe: Avanzar la seguridad de MLLM de la intención maliciosa a las consecuencias ocultas
es.summary: OOD-MMSafe introduce un benchmark y marco para avanzar la seguridad de los modelos de lenguaje multimodales enfocándose en identificar riesgos latentes en cadenas causales en lugar de intención maliciosa. El marco CASPO mejora la conciencia de consecuencias, reduciendo las tasas de fallo a alrededor del 5-7% en modelos especializados.

title: Does the Question Really Matter? Training-Free Data Selection for Vision-Language SFT
summary: CVS is a training-free data selection method that identifies high-quality vision-language training samples by measuring how questions alter the model's assessment of answer validity. The approach achieves 3-5% performance improvements while using only 10-15% of training data.
content:
arXiv:2603.09715v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Visual instruction tuning is crucial for improving vision-language large models (VLLMs). However, many samples can be solved via linguistic patterns or common-sense shortcuts, without genuine cross-modal reasoning, limiting the effectiveness of multimodal learning. Prior data selection methods often rely on costly proxy model training and focus on difficulty or diversity, failing to capture a sample's true contribution to vision-language joint reasoning. In this paper, we propose CVS, a training-free data selection method based on the insight that, for high-quality multimodal samples, introducing the question should substantially alter the model's assessment of answer validity given an image. CVS leverages a frozen VLLM as an evaluator and measures the discrepancy in answer validity with and without conditioning on the question, enabling the identification of samples that require vision-language joint reasoning while filtering semantic-conflict noise. Experiments on Vision-Flan and The Cauldron show that CVS achieves solid performance across datasets. On Vision-Flan, CVS outperforms full-data training by 3.5% and 4.8% using only 10% and 15% of the data, respectively, and remains robust on the highly heterogeneous Cauldron dataset. Moreover, CVS reduces computational cost by 17.3% and 44.4% compared to COINCIDE and XMAS.

zh.title: 问题真的重要吗？视觉语言监督微调的无训练数据选择
zh.summary: CVS是一种无训练数据选择方法,通过测量问题如何改变模型对答案有效性的评估来识别高质量的视觉语言训练样本。该方法在仅使用10-15%的训练数据的情况下实现了3-5%的性能改进。

fr.title: La question compte-t-elle vraiment ? Sélection de données sans entraînement pour la mise au point supervisée vision-langage
fr.summary: CVS est une méthode de sélection de données sans entraînement qui identifie des échantillons d'entraînement vision-langage de haute qualité en mesurant comment les questions altèrent l'évaluation de la validité des réponses par le modèle. L'approche réalise des améliorations de performance de 3-5% en n'utilisant que 10-15% des données d'entraînement.

de.title: Spielt die Frage wirklich eine Rolle? Training-freie Datenauswahl für Vision-Language SFT
de.summary: CVS ist eine trainingsfreie Datenauswahlmethode, die hochwertige Vision-Language-Trainingsstichproben identifiziert, indem gemessen wird, wie Fragen die Bewertung der Antwortgültigkeit durch das Modell verändern. Das Verfahren erreicht Leistungsverbesserungen von 3-5% bei Verwendung von nur 10-15% der Trainingsdaten.

es.title: ¿Realmente importa la pregunta? Selección de datos sin entrenamiento para la afinación supervisada de visión-lenguaje
es.summary: CVS es un método de selección de datos sin entrenamiento que identifica muestras de entrenamiento de visión-lenguaje de alta calidad midiendo cómo las preguntas alteran la evaluación del modelo sobre la validez de la respuesta. El enfoque logra mejoras de desempeño del 3-5% mientras usa solo el 10-15% de los datos de entrenamiento.

title: AutoAgent: Evolving Cognition and Elastic Memory Orchestration for Adaptive Agents
summary: AutoAgent framework enables autonomous agents to evolve cognition through experience, make real-time context-aware decisions, and efficiently orchestrate memory for long-horizon tasks. The system balances experiential learning with adaptive decision-making across dynamic environments.
content:
arXiv:2603.09716v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous agent frameworks still struggle to reconcile long-term experiential learning with real-time, context-sensitive decision-making. In practice, this gap appears as static cognition, rigid workflow dependence, and inefficient context usage, which jointly limit adaptability in open-ended and non-stationary environments. To address these limitations, we present AutoAgent, a self-evolving multi-agent framework built on three tightly coupled components: evolving cognition, on-the-fly contextual decision-making, and elastic memory orchestration. At the core of AutoAgent, each agent maintains structured prompt-level cognition over tools, self-capabilities, peer expertise, and task knowledge. During execution, this cognition is combined with live task context to select actions from a unified space that includes tool calls, LLM-based generation, and inter-agent requests. To support efficient long-horizon reasoning, an Elastic Memory Orchestrator dynamically organizes interaction history by preserving raw records, compressing redundant trajectories, and constructing reusable episodic abstractions, thereby reducing token overhead while retaining decision-critical evidence. These components are integrated through a closed-loop cognitive evolution process that aligns intended actions with observed outcomes to continuously update cognition and expand reusable skills, without external retraining. Empirical results across retrieval-augmented reasoning, tool-augmented agent benchmarks, and embodied task environments show that AutoAgent consistently improves task success, tool-use efficiency, and collaborative robustness over static and memory-augmented baselines. Overall, AutoAgent provides a unified and practical foundation for adaptive autonomous agents that must learn from experience while making reliable context-aware decisions in dynamic environments.

zh.title: AutoAgent：进化认知与自适应代理的弹性记忆编排
zh.summary: AutoAgent框架使自主代理能够通过经验演化认知，做出实时上下文感知决策，并为长期任务高效编排记忆。该系统在动态环境中平衡经验学习与自适应决策制定。

fr.title: AutoAgent: Cognition Évolutive et Orchestration de Mémoire Élastique pour les Agents Adaptatifs
fr.summary: Le framework AutoAgent permet aux agents autonomes d'évoluer la cognition par l'expérience, de prendre des décisions conscientes du contexte en temps réel, et d'orchestrer efficacement la mémoire pour les tâches à long terme. Le système équilibre l'apprentissage expérientiel avec la prise de décision adaptative dans les environnements dynamiques.

de.title: AutoAgent: Evolvierende Kognition und elastische Speicherorchestrierung für adaptive Agenten
de.summary: Das AutoAgent-Framework ermöglicht autonomen Agenten, ihre Kognition durch Erfahrung zu entwickeln, echtzeitbewusste Kontextentscheidungen zu treffen und Speicher für langfristige Aufgaben effizient zu orchestrieren. Das System balanciert erfahrungsbasiertes Lernen mit adaptiver Entscheidungsfindung in dynamischen Umgebungen.

es.title: AutoAgent: Cognición Evolutiva y Orquestación de Memoria Elástica para Agentes Adaptativos
es.summary: El framework AutoAgent permite que los agentes autónomos evolucionen cognitivamente a través de la experiencia, tomen decisiones conscientes del contexto en tiempo real y orquesten eficientemente la memoria para tareas de largo horizonte. El sistema equilibra el aprendizaje experiencial con la toma de decisiones adaptativa en entornos dinámicos.

title: World2Mind: Cognition Toolkit for Allocentric Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models
summary: World2Mind provides a training-free spatial reasoning toolkit using 3D reconstruction and allocentric spatial maps to improve multimodal models' spatial understanding. The framework enables advanced spatial reasoning even in text-only foundation models, improving performance by 5-18%.
content:
arXiv:2603.09774v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Achieving robust spatial reasoning remains a fundamental challenge for current Multimodal Foundation Models (MFMs). Existing methods either overfit statistical shortcuts via 3D grounding data or remain confined to 2D visual perception, limiting both spatial reasoning accuracy and generalization in unseen scenarios. Inspired by the spatial cognitive mapping mechanisms of biological intelligence, we propose World2Mind, a training-free spatial intelligence toolkit. At its core, World2Mind leverages 3D reconstruction and instance segmentation models to construct structured spatial cognitive maps, empowering MFMs to proactively acquire targeted spatial knowledge regarding interested landmarks and routes of interest. To provide robust geometric-topological priors, World2Mind synthesizes an Allocentric-Spatial Tree (AST) that uses elliptical parameters to model the top-down layout of landmarks accurately. To mitigate the inherent inaccuracies of 3D reconstruction, we introduce a three-stage reasoning chain comprising tool invocation assessment, modality-decoupled cue collection, and geometry-semantics interwoven reasoning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that World2Mind boosts the performance of frontier models, such as GPT-5.2, by 5%~18%. Astonishingly, relying solely on the AST-structured text, purely text-only foundation models can perform complex 3D spatial reasoning, achieving performance approaching that of advanced multimodal models.

zh.title: World2Mind：基础模型中的分配中心空间推理认知工具包
zh.summary: World2Mind提供一个无需训练的空间推理工具包，利用3D重建和分配中心空间地图来改进多模态模型的空间理解。该框架甚至在纯文本基础模型中也能实现高级空间推理，性能提升5-18%。

fr.title: World2Mind: Boîte à outils de cognition pour le raisonnement spatial allocentrique dans les modèles fondamentaux
fr.summary: World2Mind fournit une boîte à outils de raisonnement spatial sans formation utilisant la reconstruction 3D et les cartes spatiales allocentriques pour améliorer la compréhension spatiale des modèles multimodaux. Le framework permet le raisonnement spatial avancé même dans les modèles fondamentaux texte uniquement, améliorant les performances de 5-18%.

de.title: World2Mind: Cognition-Toolkit für allocentrisches räumliches Denken in Foundation Models
de.summary: World2Mind bietet ein trainingsfreies Spatial-Reasoning-Toolkit mit 3D-Rekonstruktion und allocentrischen räumlichen Karten zur Verbesserung des räumlichen Verständnisses von multimodalen Modellen. Das Framework ermöglicht fortgeschrittenes räumliches Denken auch in nur-Text-Foundation-Modellen und verbessert die Leistung um 5-18%.

es.title: World2Mind: Kit de herramientas de cognición para razonamiento espacial alocéntrico en modelos fundamentales
es.summary: World2Mind proporciona un kit de herramientas de razonamiento espacial sin entrenamiento usando reconstrucción 3D y mapas espaciales alocéntricos para mejorar la comprensión espacial de modelos multimodales. El framework permite razonamiento espacial avanzado incluso en modelos fundamentales de solo texto, mejorando el rendimiento en un 5-18%.

title: Quantifying the Necessity of Chain of Thought through Opaque Serial Depth
summary: The paper formalizes opaque serial depth to explain why Transformer-based LLMs must externalize reasoning through chain of thought mechanisms. Results suggest Mixture-of-Experts architectures have lower serial depth than dense models, affecting their reasoning externalization requirements.
content:
arXiv:2603.09786v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) tend to externalize their reasoning in their chain of thought, making the chain of thought a good target for monitoring. This is partially an inherent feature of the Transformer architecture: sufficiently long serial cognition must pass through the chain of thought (Korbak et al., 2025). We formalize this argument through the notion of opaque serial depth, given by the length of the longest computation that can be done without the use of interpretable intermediate steps like chain of thought. Given this formalization, we compute numeric upper bounds on the opaque serial depth of Gemma 3 models, as well as asymptotic results for additional architectures beyond standard LLMs. We also open-source an automated method that can calculate upper bounds on the opaque serial depth of arbitrary neural networks, and use it to demonstrate that Mixture-of-Experts models likely have lower depth than dense models. Overall, our results suggest that opaque serial depth is a useful tool for understanding the potential for models to do significant reasoning that is not externalized.

zh.title: 通过不透明串行深度量化思维链的必要性
zh.summary: 该论文将不透明串行深度形式化，以解释为什么基于Transformer的大语言模型必须通过思维链机制将推理外部化。结果表明混合专家架构的串行深度低于密集模型，影响它们的推理外部化需求。

fr.title: Quantifier la nécessité de la chaîne de pensée par la profondeur série opaque
fr.summary: L'article formalise la profondeur série opaque pour expliquer pourquoi les LLM basés sur Transformer doivent externaliser le raisonnement par des mécanismes de chaîne de pensée. Les résultats suggèrent que les architectures Mixture-of-Experts ont une profondeur série plus faible que les modèles denses, affectant leurs exigences d'externalisation du raisonnement.

de.title: Quantifizierung der Notwendigkeit von Chain of Thought durch opake serielle Tiefe
de.summary: Das Paper formalisiert opake serielle Tiefe, um zu erklären, warum Transformer-basierte LLMs das Denken durch Chain-of-Thought-Mechanismen externalisieren müssen. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass Mixture-of-Experts-Architekturen eine niedrigere serielle Tiefe als dichte Modelle haben, was ihre Anforderungen zur Externalisierung des Denkens beeinflusst.

es.title: Cuantificar la necesidad de cadena de pensamiento a través de profundidad serie opaca
es.summary: El documento formaliza la profundidad serie opaca para explicar por qué los LLM basados en Transformer deben externalizar el razonamiento a través de mecanismos de cadena de pensamiento. Los resultados sugieren que las arquitecturas Mixture-of-Experts tienen una profundidad serie más baja que los modelos densos, afectando sus requisitos de externalización del razonamiento.

title: LCA: Local Classifier Alignment for Continual Learning
summary: Researchers propose Local Classifier Alignment (LCA), a novel loss function designed to prevent catastrophic forgetting in continual learning systems. By better aligning task-specific classifiers with the adapted backbone, LCA improves both generalization and robustness across multiple tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09888v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: A fundamental requirement for intelligent systems is the ability to learn continuously under changing environments. However, models trained in this regime often suffer from catastrophic forgetting. Leveraging pre-trained models has recently emerged as a promising solution, since their generalized feature extractors enable faster and more robust adaptation. While some earlier works mitigate forgetting by fine-tuning only on the first task, this approach quickly deteriorates as the number of tasks grows and the data distributions diverge. More recent research instead seeks to consolidate task knowledge into a unified backbone, or adapting the backbone as new tasks arrive. However, such approaches may create a (potential) \textit{mismatch} between task-specific classifiers and the adapted backbone. To address this issue, we propose a novel \textit{Local Classifier Alignment} (LCA) loss to better align the classifier with backbone. Theoretically, we show that this LCA loss can enable the classifier to not only generalize well for all observed tasks, but also improve robustness. Furthermore, we develop a complete solution for continual learning, following the model merging approach and using LCA. Extensive experiments on several standard benchmarks demonstrate that our method often achieves leading performance, sometimes surpasses the state-of-the-art methods with a large margin.

zh.title: LCA：持续学习的本地分类器对齐
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了本地分类器对齐（LCA），一种新颖的损失函数，旨在防止持续学习系统中的灾难性遗忘。通过更好地将特定任务的分类器与适应的主干网络对齐，LCA在多个任务中改进了泛化性能和鲁棒性。

fr.title: LCA : Alignement des classificateurs locaux pour l'apprentissage continu
