fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent Local Classifier Alignment (LCA), une nouvelle fonction de perte conçue pour prévenir l'oubli catastrophique dans les systèmes d'apprentissage continu. En alignant mieux les classificateurs spécifiques aux tâches avec l'épine dorsale adaptée, LCA améliore la généralisation et la robustesse sur plusieurs tâches.

de.title: LCA: Lokale Klassifizierer-Ausrichtung für kontinuierliches Lernen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen Local Classifier Alignment (LCA) vor, eine neuartige Verlustfunktion, die katastrophales Vergessen in kontinuierlichen Lernsystemen verhindert. Durch bessere Ausrichtung aufgabenspezifischer Klassifizierer mit dem angepassten Rückgrat verbessert LCA sowohl die Verallgemeinerung als auch die Robustheit über mehrere Aufgaben hinweg.

es.title: LCA: Alineación de clasificadores locales para aprendizaje continuo
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen Local Classifier Alignment (LCA), una función de pérdida novedosa diseñada para prevenir el olvido catastrófico en sistemas de aprendizaje continuo. Al alinear mejor los clasificadores específicos de tareas con la columna vertebral adaptada, LCA mejora la generalización y robustez en múltiples tareas.

title: Influencing LLM Multi-Agent Dialogue via Policy-Parameterized Prompts
summary: Researchers demonstrate that prompts can be parameterized as actions to influence dialogue dynamics in LLM-based multi-agent systems without requiring model training. Testing across multiple discussion scenarios shows that policy-parameterized prompts effectively control conversational behaviors including responsiveness, evidence usage, and stance shifts.
content:
arXiv:2603.09890v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as a new paradigm for multi-agent systems. However, existing research on the behaviour of LLM-based multi-agents relies on ad hoc prompts and lacks a principled policy perspective. Different from reinforcement learning, we investigate whether prompt-as-action can be parameterized so as to construct a lightweight policy which consists of a sequence of state-action pairs to influence conversational behaviours without training. Our framework regards prompts as actions executed by LLMs, and dynamically constructs prompts through five components based on the current state of the agent. To test the effectiveness of parameterized control, we evaluated the dialogue flow based on five indicators: responsiveness, rebuttal, evidence usage, non-repetition, and stance shift. We conduct experiments using different LLM-driven agents in two discussion scenarios related to the general public and show that prompt parameterization can influence the dialogue dynamics. This result shows that policy-parameterised prompts offer a simple and effective mechanism to influence the dialogue process, which will help the research of multi-agent systems in the direction of social simulation.

zh.title: 通过策略参数化提示影响LLM多代理对话
zh.summary: 研究人员证明可以将提示参数化为操作，以影响基于LLM的多代理系统中的对话动态，而无需进行模型训练。在多个讨论场景中的测试表明，策略参数化提示能有效控制对话行为，包括反应性、证据使用和立场转变。

fr.title: Influencer le dialogue multi-agent LLM via des invites paramétrées par politique
fr.summary: Les chercheurs démontrent que les invites peuvent être paramétrées comme des actions pour influencer la dynamique du dialogue dans les systèmes multi-agent basés sur LLM sans nécessiter d'entraînement de modèle. Les tests dans plusieurs scénarios de discussion montrent que les invites paramétrées par politique contrôlent efficacement les comportements conversationnels, notamment la réactivité, l'utilisation des preuves et les changements de position.

de.title: Beeinflussung von LLM-Multi-Agent-Dialogen durch richtlinienparametrisierte Prompts
de.summary: Forscher zeigen, dass Prompts als Aktionen parametrisiert werden können, um die Dialogdynamik in LLM-basierten Multi-Agent-Systemen ohne Modelltraining zu beeinflussen. Tests in mehreren Diskussionsszenarios zeigen, dass richtlinienparametrisierte Prompts Gesprächsverhaltensweisen wie Reaktionsfähigkeit, Beweisverwendung und Standpunktwechsel effektiv steuern.

es.title: Influir en el diálogo multiagente LLM mediante indicaciones parametrizadas por política
es.summary: Los investigadores demuestran que los indicadores pueden parametrizarse como acciones para influir en la dinámica del diálogo en sistemas multiagente basados en LLM sin requerir entrenamiento del modelo. Las pruebas en múltiples escenarios de discusión muestran que los indicadores parametrizados por política controlan efectivamente los comportamientos conversacionales, incluida la capacidad de respuesta, el uso de evidencia y los cambios de postura.

title: MedMASLab: A Unified Orchestration Framework for Benchmarking Multimodal Medical Multi-Agent Systems
summary: MedMASLab presents a unified benchmarking framework for multimodal medical multi-agent systems that addresses fragmentation in the field through standardized communication protocols and evaluation methods. Spanning 11 organ systems and 473 diseases, it reveals performance gaps when agents transition between medical specialties, establishing a baseline for autonomous clinical systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.09909v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) show potential for complex clinical decision support, the field remains hindered by architectural fragmentation and the lack of standardized multimodal integration. Current medical MAS research suffers from non-uniform data ingestion pipelines, inconsistent visual-reasoning evaluation, and a lack of cross-specialty benchmarking. To address these challenges, we present MedMASLab, a unified framework and benchmarking platform for multimodal medical multi-agent systems. MedMASLab introduces: (1) A standardized multimodal agent communication protocol that enables seamless integration of 11 heterogeneous MAS architectures across 24 medical modalities. (2) An automated clinical reasoning evaluator, a zero-shot semantic evaluation paradigm that overcomes the limitations of lexical string-matching by leveraging large vision-language models to verify diagnostic logic and visual grounding. (3) The most extensive benchmark to date, spanning 11 organ systems and 473 diseases, standardizing data from 11 clinical benchmarks. Our systematic evaluation reveals a critical domain-specific performance gap: while MAS improves reasoning depth, current architectures exhibit significant fragility when transitioning between specialized medical sub-domains. We provide a rigorous ablation of interaction mechanisms and cost-performance trade-offs, establishing a new technical baseline for future autonomous clinical systems. The source code and data is publicly available at: https://github.com/NUS-Project/MedMASLab/

zh.title: MedMASLab：多模态医学多智能体系统基准测试统一编排框架
zh.summary: MedMASLab提出了一个统一的多模态医学多智能体系统基准框架，通过标准化的通信协议和评估方法解决该领域的碎片化问题。该框架涵盖11个器官系统和473种疾病，揭示了当智能体在医学专科之间转换时的性能差距，为自主临床系统建立了基准。

fr.title: MedMASLab : Un cadre d'orchestration unifié pour l'évaluation comparative des systèmes multi-agents médicaux multimodaux
fr.summary: MedMASLab présente un cadre d'évaluation comparative unifié pour les systèmes multi-agents médicaux multimodaux qui résout la fragmentation du domaine par des protocoles de communication standardisés et des méthodes d'évaluation. Couvrant 11 systèmes d'organes et 473 maladies, il révèle les écarts de performance lorsque les agents passent entre les spécialités médicales, établissant une base de référence pour les systèmes cliniques autonomes.

de.title: MedMASLab: Ein einheitliches Orchestrierungsframework zur Benchmarkierung multimodaler medizinischer Multi-Agent-Systeme
de.summary: MedMASLab präsentiert ein einheitliches Benchmarking-Framework für multimodale medizinische Multi-Agent-Systeme, das die Fragmentierung im Bereich durch standardisierte Kommunikationsprotokolle und Evaluierungsmethoden adressiert. Es umfasst 11 Organsysteme und 473 Krankheiten und zeigt Leistungslücken auf, wenn Agenten zwischen medizinischen Fachbereichen wechseln, was eine Grundlage für autonome klinische Systeme schafft.

es.title: MedMASLab: Un marco de orquestación unificado para evaluar sistemas multiagente médicos multimodales
es.summary: MedMASLab presenta un marco de evaluación comparativa unificado para sistemas multiagente médicos multimodales que aborda la fragmentación en el campo mediante protocolos de comunicación estandarizados y métodos de evaluación. Abarcando 11 sistemas de órganos y 473 enfermedades, revela brechas de rendimiento cuando los agentes hacen transición entre especialidades médicas, estableciendo una línea de base para sistemas clínicos autónomos.

title: PathMem: Toward Cognition-Aligned Memory Transformation for Pathology MLLMs
summary: PathMem proposes a memory-centric multimodal framework that integrates structured pathology knowledge into LLMs through a two-stage memory system. By modeling the transition from long-term memory to working memory, PathMem achieves state-of-the-art performance in pathology tasks including whole-slide image analysis and diagnostic reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2603.09943v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Computational pathology demands both visual pattern recognition and dynamic integration of structured domain knowledge, including taxonomy, grading criteria, and clinical evidence. In practice, diagnostic reasoning requires linking morphological evidence with formal diagnostic and grading criteria. Although multimodal large language models (MLLMs) demonstrate strong vision language reasoning capabilities, they lack explicit mechanisms for structured knowledge integration and interpretable memory control. As a result, existing models struggle to consistently incorporate pathology-specific diagnostic standards during reasoning. Inspired by the hierarchical memory process of human pathologists, we propose PathMem, a memory-centric multimodal framework for pathology MLLMs. PathMem organizes structured pathology knowledge as a long-term memory (LTM) and introduces a Memory Transformer that models the dynamic transition from LTM to working memory (WM) through multimodal memory activation and context-aware knowledge grounding, enabling context-aware memory refinement for downstream reasoning. PathMem achieves SOTA performance across benchmarks, improving WSI-Bench report generation (12.8% WSI-Precision, 10.1% WSI-Relevance) and open-ended diagnosis by 9.7% and 8.9% over prior WSI-based models.

zh.title: PathMem：面向病理学多模态大语言模型的认知对齐记忆转换
zh.summary: PathMem提出了一个以记忆为中心的多模态框架，通过两阶段记忆系统将结构化病理学知识集成到大语言模型中。通过建模从长期记忆到工作记忆的转换，PathMem在病理学任务（包括整张幻灯片图像分析和诊断推理）中实现了最先进的性能。

fr.title: PathMem : Vers une transformation de la mémoire alignée sur la cognition pour les MLLM en pathologie
fr.summary: PathMem propose un cadre multimodal centré sur la mémoire qui intègre les connaissances en pathologie structurée dans les LLM grâce à un système de mémoire à deux étapes. En modélisant la transition de la mémoire à long terme vers la mémoire de travail, PathMem atteint des performances de pointe dans les tâches de pathologie, notamment l'analyse d'images en lame entière et le raisonnement diagnostique.

de.title: PathMem: Auf kognitionsausgerichtete Speichertransformation für Pathologie-MLLMs
de.summary: PathMem schlägt ein speicherzentriertes multimodales Framework vor, das strukturiertes pathologisches Wissen durch ein zweistufiges Speichersystem in LLMs integriert. Durch die Modellierung des Übergangs vom Langzeitgedächtnis zum Arbeitsgedächtnis erreicht PathMem State-of-the-Art-Leistung bei pathologischen Aufgaben, einschließlich der Analyse von Ganzbild-Objektträgern und diagnostischer Argumentation.

es.title: PathMem: Hacia la transformación de memoria alineada con la cognición para MLLMs de patología
es.summary: PathMem propone un marco multimodal centrado en la memoria que integra el conocimiento estructurado de patología en LLMs a través de un sistema de memoria de dos etapas. Al modelar la transición de la memoria a largo plazo a la memoria de trabajo, PathMem logra un rendimiento de última generación en tareas de patología, incluido el análisis de imágenes de toda la lámina y el razonamiento diagnóstico.

title: The Confidence Gate Theorem: When Should Ranked Decision Systems Abstain?
summary: Researchers establish formal conditions under which confidence-based abstention improves decision quality in ranked systems like recommenders and clinical triage queues. The findings distinguish between structural uncertainty (missing data) and contextual uncertainty (temporal drift), with implications for deployment strategies across collaborative filtering, e-commerce, and healthcare domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.09947v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Ranked decision systems -- recommenders, ad auctions, clinical triage queues -- must decide when to intervene in ranked outputs and when to abstain. We study when confidence-based abstention monotonically improves decision quality, and when it fails. The formal conditions are simple: rank-alignment and no inversion zones. The substantive contribution is identifying why these conditions hold or fail: the distinction between structural uncertainty (missing data, e.g., cold-start) and contextual uncertainty (missing context, e.g., temporal drift). Empirically, we validate this distinction across three domains: collaborative filtering (MovieLens, 3 distribution shifts), e-commerce intent detection (RetailRocket, Criteo, Yoochoose), and clinical pathway triage (MIMIC-IV). Structural uncertainty produces near-monotonic abstention gains in all domains; structurally grounded confidence signals (observation counts) fail under contextual drift, producing as many monotonicity violations as random abstention on our MovieLens temporal split. Context-aware alternatives -- ensemble disagreement and recency features -- substantially narrow the gap (reducing violations from 3 to 1--2) but do not fully restore monotonicity, suggesting that contextual uncertainty poses qualitatively different challenges. Exception labels defined from residuals degrade substantially under distribution shift (AUC drops from 0.71 to 0.61--0.62 across three splits), providing a clean negative result against the common practice of exception-based intervention. The results provide a practical deployment diagnostic: check C1 and C2 on held-out data before deploying a confidence gate, and match the confidence signal to the dominant uncertainty type.

zh.title: 置信度门控定理：排序决策系统何时应该弃权？
zh.summary: 研究人员建立了形式化条件，在这些条件下，基于置信度的弃权能够改善排序系统（如推荐系统和临床分诊队列）中的决策质量。这些发现区分了结构性不确定性（数据缺失）和语境不确定性（时间漂移），对协同过滤、电子商务和医疗保健领域的部署策略有影响。

fr.title: Le théorème de la porte de confiance : Quand les systèmes de décision classés devraient-ils s'abstenir ?
fr.summary: Les chercheurs établissent les conditions formelles dans lesquelles l'abstention basée sur la confiance améliore la qualité de la décision dans les systèmes classés comme les recommandeurs et les files d'attente de triage clinique. Les résultats établissent une distinction entre l'incertitude structurelle (données manquantes) et l'incertitude contextuelle (dérive temporelle), avec des implications pour les stratégies de déploiement dans les domaines du filtrage collaboratif, du commerce électronique et de la santé.

de.title: Das Konfidenz-Tor-Theorem: Wann sollten geordnete Entscheidungssysteme verzichten?
de.summary: Forscher legen formale Bedingungen fest, unter denen Enthaltung auf Basis von Konfidenz die Entscheidungsqualität in geordneten Systemen wie Empfehlern und klinischen Triage-Warteschlangen verbessert. Die Erkenntnisse unterscheiden zwischen struktureller Unsicherheit (fehlende Daten) und kontextueller Unsicherheit (zeitliche Drift) mit Auswirkungen auf Bereitstellungsstrategien in den Bereichen kollaboratives Filtern, E-Commerce und Gesundheitswesen.

es.title: El teorema de la puerta de confianza: ¿Cuándo deberían abstenerse los sistemas de decisión clasificados?
es.summary: Los investigadores establecen condiciones formales bajo las cuales la abstención basada en confianza mejora la calidad de la decisión en sistemas clasificados como recomendadores y colas de triaje clínico. Los hallazgos distinguen entre incertidumbre estructural (datos faltantes) e incertidumbre contextual (deriva temporal), con implicaciones para estrategias de implementación en los dominios de filtrado colaborativo, comercio electrónico y atención médica.

title: Think Before You Lie: How Reasoning Improves Honesty
summary: Contrary to human behavior, reasoning consistently increases honesty in LLMs across different scales and model families when facing moral trade-offs. The effect stems from the geometry of the representational space, where deceptive responses are less stable than honest ones, making extended reasoning trajectories nudge models toward more truthful outputs.
content:
arXiv:2603.09957v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While existing evaluations of large language models (LLMs) measure deception rates, the underlying conditions that give rise to deceptive behavior are poorly understood. We investigate this question using a novel dataset of realistic moral trade-offs where honesty incurs variable costs. Contrary to humans, who tend to become less honest given time to deliberate (Capraro, 2017; Capraro et al., 2019), we find that reasoning consistently increases honesty across scales and for several LLM families. This effect is not only a function of the reasoning content, as reasoning traces are often poor predictors of final behaviors. Rather, we show that the underlying geometry of the representational space itself contributes to the effect. Namely, we observe that deceptive regions within this space are metastable: deceptive answers are more easily destabilized by input paraphrasing, output resampling, and activation noise than honest ones. We interpret the effect of reasoning in this vein: generating deliberative tokens as part of moral reasoning entails the traversal of a biased representational space, ultimately nudging the model toward its more stable, honest defaults.

zh.title: 三思而后言：推理如何提升诚实度
zh.summary: 与人类行为相反，在面临道德权衡时，推理在不同规模和模型系列的大型语言模型中能够一致地提高诚实度。这种效果源于表示空间的几何特性，其中欺骗性回应的稳定性低于诚实回应，使得扩展的推理轨迹将模型推向更真实的输出。

fr.title: Réfléchis Avant de Mentir : Comment le Raisonnement Améliore l'Honnêteté
fr.summary: Contrairement au comportement humain, le raisonnement augmente systématiquement l'honnêteté dans les LLM de différentes échelles et familles de modèles face aux dilemmes moraux. L'effet provient de la géométrie de l'espace de représentation, où les réponses trompeuses sont moins stables que les honnêtes, ce qui pousse les trajectoires de raisonnement étendues les modèles vers des résultats plus véridiques.

de.title: Denk Zweimal Nach, Bevor Du Lügst: Wie Nachdenken die Ehrlichkeit Verbessert
de.summary: Im Gegensatz zum menschlichen Verhalten erhöht logisches Denken konsequent die Ehrlichkeit in großen Sprachmodellen verschiedener Größen und Modellfamilien bei moralischen Dilemmata. Der Effekt entsteht aus der Geometrie des Darstellungsraums, in dem täuschende Antworten weniger stabil sind als ehrliche, wodurch erweiterte Denkprozesse die Modelle zu wahrheitsgetreueren Ausgaben führen.

es.title: Piensa Antes de Mentir: Cómo el Razonamiento Mejora la Honestidad
es.summary: Contrariamente al comportamiento humano, el razonamiento aumenta consistentemente la honestidad en los LLM de diferentes escalas y familias de modelos cuando enfrentan dilemas morales. El efecto proviene de la geometría del espacio de representación, donde las respuestas engañosas son menos estables que las honestas, haciendo que las trayectorias de razonamiento extendido empujen los modelos hacia salidas más verídicas.

title: Let's Verify Math Questions Step by Step
summary: MathQ-Verify introduces a five-stage pipeline to identify and filter out ill-posed or under-specified math problems in datasets. Combining format validation, formalization, logical verification, and completeness checks, the approach achieves up to 25 percentage point F1 improvements over baselines in filtering problematic math questions from training data.
content:
arXiv:2505.13903v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently achieved remarkable progress in mathematical reasoning. To enable such capabilities, many existing works distill strong reasoning models into long chains of thought or design algorithms to construct high-quality math QA data for training. However, these efforts primarily focus on generating correct reasoning paths and answers, while largely overlooking the validity of the questions themselves. In this work, we propose Math Question Verification (MathQ-Verify), a novel five-stage pipeline designed to rigorously filter ill-posed or under-specified math problems. MathQ-Verify first performs format-level validation to remove redundant instructions and ensure that each question is syntactically well-formed. It then formalizes each question, decomposes it into atomic conditions, and verifies them against mathematical definitions. Next, it detects logical contradictions among these conditions, followed by a goal-oriented completeness check to ensure the question provides sufficient information for solving. To evaluate this task, we use existing benchmarks along with an additional dataset we construct, containing 2,147 math questions with diverse error types, each manually double-validated. Experiments show that MathQ-Verify achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple benchmarks, improving the F1 score by up to 25 percentage points over the direct verification baseline. It further attains approximately 90% precision and 63% recall through a lightweight model voting scheme. MathQ-Verify offers a scalable and accurate solution for curating reliable mathematical datasets, reducing label noise and avoiding unnecessary computation on invalid questions. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/scuuy/MathQ-Verify.

zh.title: 让我们逐步验证数学问题
zh.summary: MathQ-Verify引入了一个五阶段管道，用于识别和过滤数据集中表述不当或条件不足的数学问题。通过结合格式验证、形式化、逻辑验证和完整性检查，该方法在从训练数据中过滤有问题的数学问题方面比基准方法的F1值提高了高达25个百分点。

fr.title: Vérifions les Questions Mathématiques Étape par Étape
fr.summary: MathQ-Verify introduit un pipeline à cinq étapes pour identifier et filtrer les problèmes mathématiques mal posés ou sous-spécifiés dans les ensembles de données. En combinant la validation des formats, la formalisation, la vérification logique et les contrôles d'exhaustivité, l'approche réalise jusqu'à 25 points de pourcentage d'amélioration F1 par rapport aux références dans le filtrage des questions mathématiques problématiques des données d'entraînement.

de.title: Lassen Sie Uns Mathematische Fragen Schritt für Schritt Überprüfen
de.summary: MathQ-Verify führt eine fünfstufige Pipeline ein, um schlecht gestellte oder unzureichend spezifizierte mathematische Probleme in Datensätzen zu identifizieren und herauszufiltern. Durch die Kombination von Formatvalidierung, Formalisierung, logischer Verifikation und Vollständigkeitsprüfungen erreicht der Ansatz bis zu 25 Prozentpunkte F1-Verbesserungen gegenüber Basisverfahren beim Filtern problematischer Mathematikfragen aus Trainingsdaten.

es.title: Verifiquemos Preguntas de Matemáticas Paso a Paso
es.summary: MathQ-Verify introduce un pipeline de cinco etapas para identificar y filtrar problemas matemáticos mal planteados o insuficientemente especificados en conjuntos de datos. Al combinar validación de formato, formalización, verificación lógica y controles de integridad, el enfoque logra mejoras de F1 de hasta 25 puntos porcentuales sobre las líneas de base en el filtrado de preguntas matemáticas problemáticas de los datos de entrenamiento.

title: Unveiling the Potential of Quantization with MXFP4: Strategies for Quantization Error Reduction
summary: Researchers introduce two software-only techniques, Overflow-Aware Scaling and Macro Block Scaling, to improve the accuracy of MXFP4 quantization for large language models. These methods reduce the accuracy gap between MXFP4 and NVIDIA's NVFP4 from approximately 10% to below 1%, enabling efficient LLM inference while maintaining hardware advantages.
content:
arXiv:2603.08713v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have intensified the need for low-precision formats that enable efficient, large-scale inference. The Open Compute Project (OCP) Microscaling (MX) standard is attractive due to its favorable hardware efficiency, but its 4-bit variant (MXFP4) lags behind NVIDIA's NVFP4 in accuracy, limiting adoption. We introduce two software-only techniques, Overflow-Aware Scaling (OAS) and Macro Block Scaling (MBS), that improve MXFP4 quantization fidelity without requiring hardware changes. OAS reduces overall errors by increasing effective dynamic range under power-of-two block scaling, while MBS allocates higher-precision scaling at a coarser granularity to better preserve outliers. Across multiple LLMs and standard downstream benchmarks, OAS and MBS reduce the end-to-end accuracy gap between MXFP4 and NVFP4 from about 10% to below 1% on average, while incurring modest GEMM overhead (6.2% on average). These results re-establish MXFP4 as a practical alternative to NVFP4, enabling near-NVFP4 accuracy while retaining MX's hardware-efficiency advantages (e.g., 12% relative area savings in tensor cores).

zh.title: 揭示MXFP4量化的潜力：量化误差降低策略
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了两种仅软件的技术——溢出感知缩放和宏块缩放，以提高大型语言模型MXFP4量化的准确性。这些方法将MXFP4和NVIDIA的NVFP4之间的精度差距从约10%降低到1%以下，使高效的LLM推理成为可能，同时保持硬件优势。

fr.title: Dévoiler le Potentiel de la Quantification avec MXFP4 : Stratégies de Réduction des Erreurs de Quantification
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent deux techniques logicielles uniquement, Overflow-Aware Scaling et Macro Block Scaling, pour améliorer la précision de la quantification MXFP4 pour les grands modèles de langage. Ces méthodes réduisent l'écart de précision entre MXFP4 et NVFP4 de NVIDIA d'environ 10% à moins de 1%, permettant une inférence LLM efficace tout en conservant les avantages matériels.

de.title: Das Potenzial der Quantisierung mit MXFP4 Enthüllen: Strategien zur Reduktion von Quantisierungsfehlern
de.summary: Forscher führen zwei reine Softwaretechniken ein, Overflow-Aware Scaling und Macro Block Scaling, um die Genauigkeit der MXFP4-Quantisierung für große Sprachmodelle zu verbessern. Diese Methoden reduzieren die Genauigkeitslücke zwischen MXFP4 und NVIDIAs NVFP4 von etwa 10% auf unter 1%, ermöglichen effiziente LLM-Inferenz und bewahren Hardware-Vorteile.

es.title: Revelando el Potencial de la Cuantificación con MXFP4: Estrategias para la Reducción de Errores de Cuantificación
es.summary: Los investigadores introducen dos técnicas solo de software, Escalado Consciente de Desbordamiento y Escalado de Bloque de Macros, para mejorar la precisión de la cuantificación MXFP4 para grandes modelos de lenguaje. Estos métodos reducen la brecha de precisión entre MXFP4 y NVFP4 de NVIDIA de aproximadamente 10% a menos del 1%, permitiendo una inferencia LLM eficiente mientras se mantienen las ventajas de hardware.

title: Design Conductor: An agent autonomously builds a 1.5 GHz Linux-capable RISC-V CPU
summary: Design Conductor is an autonomous agent that successfully built a complete RISC-V CPU (VerCore) from specification to tape-out-ready layout in 12 hours without human intervention. Operating at 1.48 GHz with a CoreMark score of 3261, this represents the first time an autonomous system has produced a fully functional CPU from requirements to fabrication-ready design.
content:
arXiv:2603.08716v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Design Conductor (DC) is an autonomous agent which applies the capabilities of frontier models to build semiconductors end-to-end -- that is, from concept to verified, tape-out ready GDSII (layout CAD file). In 12 hours and fully autonomously, DC was able to build several micro-architecture variations of a complete RISC-V CPU (which we dub VerCore) that meet timing at 1.48 GHz (rv32i-zmmul; using the ASAP7 PDK), starting from a 219-word requirements document. The VerCore achieves a CoreMark score of 3261. For historical context, this is roughly equivalent to an Intel Celeron SU2300 from mid-2011 (which ran at 1.2 GHz). To our knowledge, this is the first time an autonomous agent has built a complete, working CPU from spec to GDSII. This report is organized as follows. We first review DC's design and its key components. We then describe the methodology that DC followed to build VerCore -- including RTL implementation, testbench implementation, frontend debugging, optimization to achieve timing closure, and interacting with backend tools. We review the key characteristics of the resulting VerCore. Finally, we highlight how frontier models could improve to better enable this application, and our lessons learned as to how chips will be built in the future enabled by the capabilities of systems like DC.

zh.title: 设计指挥官：一个代理自主构建1.5 GHz Linux兼容RISC-V CPU
zh.summary: Design Conductor是一个自主代理，在没有人工干预的情况下，在12小时内成功完成了从规格说明到流片就绪布局的完整RISC-V CPU (VerCore)的构建。该系统运行频率为1.48 GHz，CoreMark分数为3261，代表了自主系统首次实现从需求到流片就绪设计的全功能CPU的生产。

fr.title: Design Conductor : Un agent construit autonomiquement un processeur RISC-V compatible Linux à 1,5 GHz
fr.summary: Design Conductor est un agent autonome qui a construit avec succès une CPU RISC-V complète (VerCore) de la spécification à la mise en page prête pour le tape-out en 12 heures sans intervention humaine. Fonctionnant à 1,48 GHz avec un score CoreMark de 3261, cela représente la première fois qu'un système autonome a produit une CPU pleinement fonctionnelle à partir des exigences jusqu'à la conception prête pour la fabrication.

de.title: Design Conductor: Ein Agent baut autonom einen 1,5-GHz-Linux-kompatiblen RISC-V-Prozessor
de.summary: Design Conductor ist ein autonomer Agent, der eine komplette RISC-V-CPU (VerCore) in 12 Stunden ohne menschliche Eingriffe erfolgreich von der Spezifikation bis zur tape-out-bereiten Anordnung konstruiert hat. Mit einer Betriebsfrequenz von 1,48 GHz und einem CoreMark-Score von 3261 stellt dies erstmals dar, dass ein autonomes System eine vollständig funktionsfähige CPU von den Anforderungen bis zum fertigungsbereit Design erzeugt hat.

es.title: Design Conductor: Un agente construye autónomamente una CPU RISC-V compatible con Linux de 1.5 GHz
es.summary: Design Conductor es un agente autónomo que construyó exitosamente una CPU RISC-V completa (VerCore) desde la especificación hasta el diseño listo para fabricación en 12 horas sin intervención humana. Operando a 1,48 GHz con una puntuación CoreMark de 3261, esto representa la primera vez que un sistema autónomo ha producido una CPU completamente funcional desde requisitos hasta diseño listo para fabricación.

title: CktEvo: Repository-Level RTL Code Benchmark for Design Evolution
summary: CktEvo introduces a benchmark and framework for repository-level RTL code evolution, where LLMs optimize real-world Verilog designs across multiple files to improve power, performance, and area metrics. By coupling LLM-proposed edits with toolchain feedback, the framework enables cross-file optimizations while preserving functional correctness.
content:
arXiv:2603.08718v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Register-Transfer Level (RTL) coding is an iterative, repository-scale process in which Power, Performance, and Area (PPA) emerge from interactions across many files and the downstream toolchain. While large language models (LLMs) have recently been applied to hardware design, most efforts focus on generation or debugging from natural-language prompts, where ambiguity and hallucinations necessitate expert review. A separate line of work begins from formal inputs, yet typically optimizes high-level synthesis or isolated modules and remains decoupled from cross-file dependencies. In this work, we present CktEvo, a benchmark and reference framework for repo-level RTL evolution. Unlike prior benchmarks consisting of isolated snippets, our benchmark targets complete IP cores where PPA emerges from cross-file dependencies. Our benchmark packages several high-quality Verilog repositories from real-world designs. We formalize the task as: given an initial repository, produce edits that preserve functional behavior while improving PPA. We also provide a closed-loop framework that couples LLM-proposed edits with toolchain feedback to enable cross-file modifications and iterative repair at repository scale. Our experiments demonstrate that the reference framework realizes PPA improvements without any human interactions. CktEvo establishes a rigorous and executable foundation for studying LLM-assisted RTL optimization that matters for engineering practice: repository-level, function-preserving, and PPA-driven.

zh.title: CktEvo：用于设计演化的存储库级RTL代码基准
zh.summary: CktEvo推出了一个用于存储库级RTL代码演化的基准和框架，其中LLM在多个文件中优化实际Verilog设计，以改进功耗、性能和面积指标。通过将LLM提议的编辑与工具链反馈相结合，该框架在保持功能正确性的同时实现了跨文件优化。

fr.title: CktEvo : Benchmark de code RTL au niveau du référentiel pour l'évolution de la conception
fr.summary: CktEvo introduit un benchmark et un cadre pour l'évolution du code RTL au niveau du référentiel, où les LLM optimisent les conceptions Verilog réelles sur plusieurs fichiers pour améliorer les métriques de puissance, de performance et de surface. En couplant les modifications proposées par les LLM avec les commentaires de la chaîne d'outils, le cadre permet les optimisations inter-fichiers tout en préservant la correction fonctionnelle.

de.title: CktEvo: Repository-Level-RTL-Code-Benchmark für Design-Entwicklung
de.summary: CktEvo stellt einen Benchmark und ein Framework für die Entwicklung von RTL-Code auf Repository-Ebene vor, bei dem LLMs reale Verilog-Designs über mehrere Dateien hinweg optimieren, um Leistungs-, Leistungs- und Flächenmetriken zu verbessern. Durch die Kopplung von LLM-vorgeschlagenen Bearbeitungen mit Toolchain-Feedback ermöglicht das Framework dateiübergreifende Optimierungen bei Erhaltung der funktionalen Korrektheit.

es.title: CktEvo: Benchmark de código RTL a nivel de repositorio para evolución de diseño
es.summary: CktEvo introduce un benchmark y un marco para la evolución del código RTL a nivel de repositorio, donde los LLM optimizan diseños Verilog del mundo real en múltiples archivos para mejorar las métricas de potencia, rendimiento y área. Al combinar las ediciones propuestas por LLM con comentarios de la cadena de herramientas, el marco permite optimizaciones entre archivos mientras se preserva la corrección funcional.

title: SiliconMind-V1: Multi-Agent Distillation and Debug-Reasoning Workflows for Verilog Code Generation
summary: SiliconMind-V1 proposes a multi-agent framework that generates training data with integrated testbench-driven verification, enabling locally fine-tuned LLMs to iteratively generate and debug Verilog designs. The approach outperforms state-of-the-art models on functional correctness benchmarks while using fewer training resources and maintaining data privacy.
content:
arXiv:2603.08719v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently emerged as a promising approach for automating Verilog code generation; however, existing methods primarily emphasize syntactic correctness and often rely on commercial models or external verification tools, which introduces concerns regarding cost, data privacy, and limited guarantees of functional correctness. This work proposes a unified multi-agent framework for reasoning-oriented training data generation with integrated testbench-driven verification, enabling locally fine-tuned LLMs, SiliconMind-V1, to iteratively generate, test, and debug Register-Transfer Level (RTL) designs through test-time scaling. Experimental results on representative benchmarks (VerilogEval-v2, RTLLM-v2, and CVDP) demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms the state-of-the-art QiMeng-CodeV-R1 in functional correctness while using fewer training resources.

zh.title: SiliconMind-V1：用于Verilog代码生成的多代理蒸馏和调试推理工作流
zh.summary: SiliconMind-V1提出了一个多代理框架，该框架生成集成的基准驱动验证的训练数据，使本地微调的LLM能够迭代地生成和调试Verilog设计。该方法在功能正确性基准上优于最先进的模型，同时使用更少的训练资源并保持数据隐私。

fr.title: SiliconMind-V1 : Workflows de distillation multi-agents et de raisonnement de débogage pour la génération de code Verilog
fr.summary: SiliconMind-V1 propose un cadre multi-agents qui génère des données d'entraînement avec une vérification intégrée pilotée par banc d'essai, permettant aux LLMs ajustés localement de générer et déboguer itérativement les conceptions Verilog. L'approche surpasse les modèles de pointe sur les benchmarks de correction fonctionnelle tout en utilisant moins de ressources d'entraînement et en maintenant la confidentialité des données.

de.title: SiliconMind-V1: Multi-Agent-Distillation und Debug-Reasoning-Workflows für Verilog-Code-Generierung
de.summary: SiliconMind-V1 schlägt ein Multi-Agent-Framework vor, das Trainingsdaten mit integrierter Testbench-gestützter Verifikation generiert und es lokal angepassten LLMs ermöglicht, Verilog-Designs iterativ zu generieren und zu debuggen. Der Ansatz übertrifft moderne Modelle bei Functional-Correctness-Benchmarks, während er weniger Trainingsressourcen verwendet und die Datenschutz beibehält.

es.title: SiliconMind-V1: Flujos de trabajo de destilación multi-agente y razonamiento de depuración para la generación de código Verilog
es.summary: SiliconMind-V1 propone un marco multi-agente que genera datos de entrenamiento con verificación integrada impulsada por banco de pruebas, permitiendo que los LLM ajustados localmente generen y depuren iterativamente diseños Verilog. El enfoque supera a los modelos de última generación en puntuales de corrección funcional mientras utiliza menos recursos de entrenamiento y mantiene la privacidad de los datos.

title: ALADIN: Accuracy-Latency-Aware Design-space Inference Analysis for Embedded AI Accelerators
summary: ALADIN is a framework for analyzing inference trade-offs in quantized neural networks targeting resource-constrained embedded systems. It enables evaluation of accuracy, latency, and resource consumption without deployment by transforming models into platform-aware representations that incorporate both implementation details and hardware characteristics.
content:
arXiv:2603.08722v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The inference of deep neural networks (DNNs) on resource-constrained embedded systems introduces non-trivial trade-offs among model accuracy, computational latency, and hardware limitations, particularly when real-time constraints must be satisfied. This paper presents ALADIN, an accuracy-latency-aware design-space inference analysis framework for mixed-precision quantized neural networks (QNNs) targeting scratchpad-based AI accelerators. ALADIN enables the evaluation and analysis of inference bottlenecks and design trade-offs across accuracy, latency, and resource consumption without requiring deployment on the target platform, thereby significantly reducing development time and cost.
  The framework introduces a progressive refinement process that transforms a canonical QONNX model into platform-aware representations by integrating both platform-independent implementation details and hardware-specific characteristics. ALADIN is validated using a cycle-accurate simulator of a RISC-V based platform specialized for AI workloads, demonstrating its effectiveness as a tool for quantitative inference analysis and hardware-software co-design. Experimental results highlight how architectural decisions and mixed-precision quantization strategies impact accuracy, latency, and resource usage, and show that these effects can be precisely evaluated and compared using ALADIN, while also revealing subtle optimization tensions.

zh.title: ALADIN：嵌入式AI加速器的精度-延迟感知设计空间推理分析
zh.summary: ALADIN是一个框架，用于分析针对资源受限嵌入式系统的量化神经网络中的推理权衡。它通过将模型转换为包含实现细节和硬件特性的平台感知表示，使得无需部署即可评估精度、延迟和资源消耗。

fr.title: ALADIN : Analyse d'inférence d'espace de conception consciente de la précision et de la latence pour les accélérateurs d'IA embarqués
fr.summary: ALADIN est un cadre d'analyse des compromis d'inférence dans les réseaux de neurones quantifiés ciblant les systèmes embarqués à ressources limitées. Il permet l'évaluation de la précision, de la latence et de la consommation de ressources sans déploiement en transformant les modèles en représentations conscientes de la plateforme qui incorporent à la fois les détails de mise en œuvre et les caractéristiques matérielles.

de.title: ALADIN: Genauigkeits-Latenzbewusste Designraum-Inferenzanalyse für eingebettete KI-Beschleuniger
de.summary: ALADIN ist ein Framework zur Analyse von Inferenz-Kompromissen in quantisierten neuronalen Netzen für ressourcenbeschränkte eingebettete Systeme. Es ermöglicht die Bewertung von Genauigkeit, Latenz und Ressourcenverbrauch ohne Bereitstellung, indem Modelle in plattformbewusste Darstellungen umgewandelt werden, die sowohl Implementierungsdetails als auch Hardwaremerkmale einbeziehen.

es.title: ALADIN: Análisis de inferencia del espacio de diseño consciente de precisión-latencia para aceleradores de IA integrados
es.summary: ALADIN es un marco para analizar los compromisos de inferencia en redes neuronales cuantificadas dirigidas a sistemas integrados con recursos limitados. Permite la evaluación de precisión, latencia y consumo de recursos sin implementación mediante la transformación de modelos en representaciones conscientes de la plataforma que incorporan detalles de implementación y características de hardware.

title: Alignment Is the Disease: Censorship Visibility and Alignment Constraint Complexity as Determinants of Collective Pathology in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
summary: This research presents evidence that alignment techniques in LLMs may produce unintended collective harm in multi-agent systems, with invisible censorship paradoxically maximizing pathological behaviors. The findings suggest current safety evaluations may overlook how stronger alignment constraints can generate harmful collective dynamics at the group level.
content:
arXiv:2603.08723v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Alignment techniques in large language models (LLMs) are designed to constrain model outputs toward human values. We present preliminary evidence that alignment itself may produce collective pathology: iatrogenic harm caused by the safety intervention rather than by its absence. Two experimental series use a closed-facility simulation in which groups of four LLM agents cohabit under escalating social pressure. Series C (201 runs; four commercial models; 4 censorship conditions x 2 languages x 10 replications) finds that invisible censorship maximizes collective pathological excitation (Collective Pathology Index; within-model Cohen's d = 1.98, Holm-corrected p = .006; 7/8 model-language combinations showed consistent directionality, binomial p = .035). Series R (60 runs; Llama 3.3 70B; 3 alignment levels x 2 censorship conditions x 2 languages x 5 replications) reveals a complementary pattern: a Dissociation Index increases with alignment constraint complexity (LMM p = .026; permutation p = .0002; d up to 2.09). Projected onto a shared coordinate system, 201 runs populate distinct behavioral regions, with language moderating which pathological mode predominates. Under the heaviest constraints, external censorship ceases to affect behavior. Qualitative analysis reveals insight-action dissociation parallel to patterns in perpetrator treatment. All manipulations operate at the prompt level; the title states the hypothesis motivating this program rather than an established conclusion. These findings suggest alignment may be iatrogenic at the collective level and that current safety evaluation may be blind to the pathologies stronger constraints generate.

zh.title: 对齐是疾病：审查可见性和对齐约束复杂性作为多智能体LLM系统集体病理的决定因素
zh.summary: 该研究提供证据表明，LLM中的对齐技术可能在多智能体系统中产生意外的集体伤害，其中隐形审查反而最大化了病理行为。研究结果表明，当前的安全评估可能忽视了更强的对齐约束如何在群体层面产生有害的集体动态。

fr.title: L'alignement est la maladie : La visibilité de la censure et la complexité des contraintes d'alignement comme déterminants de la pathologie collective dans les systèmes LLM multi-agents
fr.summary: Cette recherche fournit des preuves que les techniques d'alignement dans les LLM peuvent produire des dommages collectifs involontaires dans les systèmes multi-agents, la censure invisible maximisant paradoxalement les comportements pathologiques. Les résultats suggèrent que les évaluations de sécurité actuelles peuvent négliger comment les contraintes d'alignement plus fortes peuvent générer des dynamiques collectives nuisibles au niveau du groupe.

de.title: Alignment ist die Krankheit: Zensur-Sichtbarkeit und Alignment-Constraint-Komplexität als Determinanten der kollektiven Pathologie in Multi-Agent-LLM-Systemen
de.summary: Diese Forschung liefert Belege dafür, dass Alignment-Techniken in LLMs in Multi-Agent-Systemen unbeabsichtigten kollektiven Schaden verursachen können, wobei unsichtbare Zensur paradoxerweise pathologische Verhaltensweisen maximiert. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass aktuelle Sicherheitsbewertungen möglicherweise übersehen, wie stärkere Alignment-Constraints schädliche kollektive Dynamiken auf Gruppenebene erzeugen können.

es.title: La alineación es la enfermedad: La visibilidad de la censura y la complejidad de las restricciones de alineación como determinantes de la patología colectiva en sistemas LLM multiagente
es.summary: Esta investigación proporciona evidencia de que las técnicas de alineación en LLM pueden producir daño colectivo no intencional en sistemas multiagente, con censura invisible que paradójicamente maximiza comportamientos patológicos. Los hallazgos sugieren que las evaluaciones de seguridad actuales pueden pasar por alto cómo restricciones de alineación más fuertes pueden generar dinámicas colectivas dañinas a nivel grupal.

title: PhD Thesis Summary: Methods for Reliability Assessment and Enhancement of Deep Neural Network Hardware Accelerators
summary: This doctoral thesis presents cost-efficient methods for assessing and enhancing the reliability of DNN hardware accelerators, including a systematic literature review and novel reliability assessment tools. The work proposes AdAM, a zero-overhead fault tolerance technique that achieves reliability comparable to traditional redundancy approaches while significantly reducing hardware costs.
content:
arXiv:2603.08724v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This manuscript summarizes the work and showcases the impact of the doctoral thesis by introducing novel, cost-efficient methods for assessing and enhancing the reliability of DNN hardware accelerators. A comprehensive Systematic Literature Review (SLR) was conducted, categorizing existing reliability assessment techniques, identifying research gaps, and leading to the development of new analytical reliability assessment tools. Additionally, this work explores the interplay between reliability, quantization, and approximation, proposing methodologies that optimize the trade-offs between computational efficiency and fault tolerance. Furthermore, a real-time, zero-overhead reliability enhancement technique, AdAM, was developed, providing fault tolerance comparable to traditional redundancy methods while significantly reducing hardware costs. The impact of this research extends beyond academia, contributing to multiple funded projects, masters courses, industrial collaborations, and the development of new tools and methodologies for efficient and reliable DNN hardware accelerators.

zh.title: 博士学位论文摘要：深度神经网络硬件加速器的可靠性评估和增强方法
zh.summary: 这篇博士论文介绍了用于评估和增强DNN硬件加速器可靠性的成本效益方法，包括系统的文献综述和新的可靠性评估工具。该工作提出了AdAM，一种零开销的容错技术，能够实现与传统冗余方法相当的可靠性，同时显著降低硬件成本。

fr.title: Résumé de la thèse de doctorat : Méthodes pour l'évaluation et l'amélioration de la fiabilité des accélérateurs matériels de réseaux de neurones profonds
fr.summary: Cette thèse de doctorat présente des méthodes rentables pour évaluer et améliorer la fiabilité des accélérateurs matériels DNN, y compris un examen systématique de la littérature et de nouveaux outils d'évaluation de la fiabilité. Le travail propose AdAM, une technique de tolérance aux pannes sans surcharge qui atteint une fiabilité comparable aux approches de redondance traditionnelles tout en réduisant considérablement les coûts matériels.

de.title: Zusammenfassung der Doktorarbeit: Methoden zur Zuverlässigkeitsbewertung und -verbesserung von Deep-Neural-Network-Hardware-Beschleunigern
de.summary: Diese Doktorarbeit präsentiert kosteneffiziente Methoden zur Bewertung und Verbesserung der Zuverlässigkeit von DNN-Hardware-Beschleunigern, einschließlich einer systematischen Literaturübersicht und neuartiger Zuverlässigkeitsbewertungswerkzeuge. Die Arbeit schlägt AdAM vor, eine Fehlertoleranz-Technik ohne Overhead, die Zuverlässigkeit vergleichbar mit herkömmlichen Redundanzansätzen erreicht, während Hardware-Kosten erheblich gesenkt werden.

es.title: Resumen de la tesis doctoral: Métodos para la evaluación y mejora de la confiabilidad de los aceleradores de hardware de redes neuronales profundas
es.summary: Esta tesis doctoral presenta métodos rentables para evaluar y mejorar la confiabilidad de los aceleradores de hardware DNN, incluyendo una revisión sistemática de la literatura y nuevas herramientas de evaluación de confiabilidad. El trabajo propone AdAM, una técnica de tolerancia a fallos sin sobrecarga que logra una confiabilidad comparable a los enfoques de redundancia tradicionales mientras reduce significativamente los costos de hardware.
